1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Alif-Lam-Meem. (Alphabets of the Arabic language – Allah and to whomever He reveals, know their precise meanings.)

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Alif-Lam-Mim. [These letters are one of the miracles of the Quran, and none but Allah (Alone) knows their meanings.]

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: Do men think they will get away by saying: "We believe," and will not be tried?

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: Do men fancy that they will be left just upon their declaring, “We believe”, and they will not be tested?

2.author: Arberry
2.text: Do the people reckon that they will be left to say 'We believe,' and will not be tried?

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: Bethink men that they shall be left alone because they say: we believe; and that they shall not be tempted?

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: Do people think that they will be left alone because they say: "We believe," and will not be tested.

2.author: Itani
2.text: Have the people supposed that they will be left alone to say, “We believe,” without being put to the test?

2.author: Maududi
2.text: Do people think that they will be let go merely by saying: “We believe,” and that they will not be tested,

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: Do people think that they will be left alone because they say: "We believe," and will not be tested.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: Do men imagine that they will be left (at ease) because they say, We believe, and will not be tested with affliction?

2.author: Qarai
2.text: Do the people suppose that they will be let off because they say, ‘We have faith,’ and they will not be tested?

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: Do people think that they are left alone by saying: 'We are believers' and will not be tried?

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: Do the people think that they will be left to say, "We believe" and they will not be tried?

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: Do people think they will not be tested because they say, "We have faith?"

2.author: Shakir
2.text: Do men think that they will be left alone on saying, We believe, and not be tried?

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: Do people think that once they say, "We believe," they will be left alone and not be put to the test?

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: Do men think that they will be left alone on saying, "We believe", and that they will not be tested?

2.author: Transliteration
2.text: Ahasiba alnnasu an yutrakoo an yaqooloo amanna wahum la yuftanoona

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: We had tried those who were before them so that God knew who spoke the truth, and who were liars.

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: We indeed tested those before them – so Allah will surely test the truthful, and will surely test the liars.

3.author: Arberry
3.text: We certainly tried those that were before them, and assuredly God knows those who speak truly, and assuredly He knows the liars.

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: And assuredly We have tempted those who were before them. So Allah will surely know those who are true and He will surely know the liars.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: And We indeed tested those who were before them. And Allah will certainly make (it) known (the truth of) those who are true, and will certainly make (it) known (the falsehood of) those who are liars, (although Allah knows all that before putting them to test).

3.author: Itani
3.text: We have tested those before them. God will surely know the truthful, and He will surely know the liars.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: for We indeed tested those who went before them? Allah will most certainly ascertain those who spoke the truth and those who lied.

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: And We indeed tested those who were before them so that Allah will indeed know those who are true, and He will know those who are liars.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: Lo! We tested those who were before you. Thus Allah knoweth those who are sincere, and knoweth those who feign.

3.author: Qarai
3.text: Certainly We tested those who were before them. So Allah shall surely ascertain those who are truthful, and He shall surely ascertain the liars.

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: We tried those who have gone before them. Allah knows those who are truthful and those who lie.

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: But We have certainly tried those before them, and Allah will surely make evident those who are truthful, and He will surely make evident the liars.

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: We had certainly tried those who lived before them to make sure who were truthful in their faith and who were liars.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: And certainly We tried those before them, so Allah will certainly know those who are true and He will certainly know the liars.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: We certainly tried those who have gone before them, so God will certainly distinguish between those who are truthful and those who are lying.

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: We did test those before them, and Allah will certainly know those who are true from those who are false.

3.author: Transliteration
3.text: Walaqad fatanna allatheena min qablihim falayaAAlamanna Allahu allatheena sadaqoo walayaAAlamanna alkathibeena

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: Do those who do evil think that they will get the better of Us? How bad is the judgement that they make!

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: Or do they who commit evil deeds imagine that they can escape from Us? What an evil judgement they impose!

4.author: Arberry
4.text: Or do they reckon, those who do evil deeds, that they will outstrip Us? Ill they judge!

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: Or bethink those who work ill deeds that they shall outstrip Us? lll do they judge!

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: Or those who do evil deeds think that they can outstrip Us (i.e. escape Our Punishment)? Evil is that which they judge!

4.author: Itani
4.text: Or do those who commit sins think they can fool Us? Terrible is their opinion!

4.author: Maududi
4.text: Do the evil-doers suppose that they will get the better of Us? How evil is their judgement!

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: Or think those who do evil deeds that they can outstrip Us Evil is that which they judge!

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: Or do those who do ill-deeds imagine that they can outstrip Us? Evil (for them) is that which they decide.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: Do those who commit misdeeds suppose that they can outmaneuver Us? Evil is the judgement that they make.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: Or do the evildoers think that they will outstrip Us? How evil is their judgment!

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: Or do those who do evil deeds think they can outrun Us? Evil is what they judge.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: Do the evil-doers think they can escape Us? How terrible is their judgment?

4.author: Shakir
4.text: Or do they who work evil think that they will escape Us? Evil is it that they judge!

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: Do those who do evil imagine that they will escape Us? How ill they judge!

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: Do those who practise evil think that they will get the better of Us? Evil is their judgment!

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: Am hasiba allatheena yaAAmaloona alssayyiati an yasbiqoona saa ma yahkumoona

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: He who hopes to meet God (should know) that God's appointed time will surely come. He is all-hearing and all-knowing.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: Whoever expects to meet Allah – then indeed the time appointed by Allah will come; and He is the All Hearing, the All Knowing.

5.author: Arberry
5.text: Whoso looks to encounter God, God's term is coming; He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: Whosoever hopeth for the meeting with Allah, then Allah's term is surely coming, and He is the Hearer, the Knower.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: Whoever hopes for the Meeting with Allah, then Allah's Term is surely coming. and He is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower.

5.author: Itani
5.text: Whoever looks forward to the meeting with God—the appointed time of God is coming. He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: Let him who looks forward to meeting Allah know that Allah's appointed term will surely come to pass. He is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: Whoever hopes in meeting with Allah, then Allah's term is surely coming, and He is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: Whoso looketh forward to the meeting with Allah (let him know that) Allah's reckoning is surely nigh, and He is the Hearer, the Knower.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: Whoever expects to encounter Allah [should know that] Allah’s [appointed] time will indeed come, and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: He who hopes to meet Allah (must know) that the term of Allah is coming. He is the Hearer, the Knower.

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: Whoever should hope for the meeting with Allah - indeed, the term decreed by Allah is coming. And He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: Let those who have the desire to be in the presence of God on the Day of Judgment know that their day will certainly be coming. God is All-Hearing and All-Knowing.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Whoever hopes to meet Allah, the term appointed by Allah will then most surely come; and He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: He who hopes to meet God should know that God's appointed hour is sure to come. He is the All Hearing, the All Knowing.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: For those whose hopes are in the meeting with Allah (in the Hereafter, let them strive); for the term (appointed) by Allah is surely coming and He hears and knows (all things).

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: Man kana yarjoo liqaa Allahi fainna ajala Allahi laatin wahuwa alssameeAAu alAAaleemu

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: He who strives does so for himself. Verily God is independent of the creatures of the world.

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: And whoever strives in Allah's cause, strives only for his own benefit; indeed Allah is Independent of the entire creation.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: Whosoever struggles, struggles only to his own gain; surely God is All-sufficient nor needs any being.

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: And whosoever strive, striveth only for himself: verily Allah is Independent of the worlds.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: And whosoever strives, he strives only for himself. Verily, Allah is free of all wants from the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns, and all that exists).

6.author: Itani
6.text: Whoever strives, strives only for himself. God is Independent of the beings.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: Whosoever strives (in the cause of Allah) does so to his own good. Surely Allah stands in no need of anyone in the whole Universe.

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: And whosoever strives, he strives only for himself. Verily, Allah stands not in need of any of the creatures.

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: And whosoever striveth, striveth only for himself, for lo! Allah is altogether Independent of (His) creatures.

6.author: Qarai
6.text: Whoever strives, strives only for his own sake. Indeed Allah has no need of the creatures.

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: He who strives, strives for himself. Allah is the Rich, independent of the worlds.

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: And whoever strives only strives for [the benefit of] himself. Indeed, Allah is free from need of the worlds.

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: Whoever strives hard should know that it is for his own good. God is independent of the whole world.

6.author: Shakir
6.text: And whoever strives hard, he strives only for his own soul; most surely Allah is Self-sufficient, above (need of) the worlds.

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: And whoever strives, strives only for himself, God is independent of all His creation,

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: And if any strive (with might and main), they do so for their own souls: for Allah is free of all needs from all creation.

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Waman jahada fainnama yujahidu linafsihi inna Allaha laghaniyyun AAani alAAalameena

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: We shall pardon the sinful deeds of those who believe and do the right, and give them a reward better than their deeds.

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: And those who accepted faith and did good deeds – We will indeed relieve their sins and reward them for the best of their deeds.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: And those who believe, and do righteous deeds, We shall surely acquit them of their evil deeds, and shall recompense them the best of what they were doing.

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: And whosoever believe and work righteous works, We shall purge away from them their ill-deeds and shall recompense them the best of that which they have been working.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: Those who believe [in the Oneness of Allah (Monotheism) and in Messenger Muhammad SAW, and do not apostate because of the harm they receive from the polytheists], and do righteous good deeds, surely, We shall remit from them their evil deeds and shall reward them according to the best of that which they used to do.

7.author: Itani
7.text: Those who believe and do righteous deeds—We will remit their sins, and We will reward them according to the best of what they used to do.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: Those who believe and do good deeds, We shall cleanse them of their evil deeds and reward them according to the best of their deeds.

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: Those who believe, and do righteous good deeds, surely, We shall expiate from them their evil deeds and We shall indeed reward them according to the best of that which they used to do.

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: And as for those who believe and do good works, We shall remit from them their evil deeds and shall repay them the best that they did.

7.author: Qarai
7.text: As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, We will absolve them of their misdeeds and We will surely reward them by the best of what they used to do.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: As for those who believe and do good works, We shall acquit them of their sins, and recompense them with the best for what they were doing.

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: And those who believe and do righteous deeds - We will surely remove from them their misdeeds and will surely reward them according to the best of what they used to do.

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: We shall expiate the sins of the righteously striving believers and shall reward them better than their deeds.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: And (as for) those who believe and do good, We will most certainly do away with their evil deeds and We will most certainly reward them the best of what they did.

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: as for those who have faith and do good works, We shall certainly cleanse them of their evil deeds and reward them according to the best of their actions.

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: Those who believe and work righteous deeds,- from them shall We blot out all evil (that may be) in them, and We shall reward them according to the best of their deeds.

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Waallatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati lanukaffiranna AAanhum sayyiatihim walanajziyannahum ahsana allathee kanoo yaAAmaloona

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: We have enjoined on man to be good to his parents; but if they try to make you associate with Me that of which you have no knowledge, then do not obey them. You have to come back to Us, when I will tell you what you used to do.

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: And upon man We ordained kindness towards parents; and if they strive to make you ascribe a partner with Me, about which you do not have any knowledge, then do not obey them; towards Me only is your return and I will tell you what you used to do.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: We have charged man, that he be kind to his parents; but if they strive with thee to make thee associate with Me that whereof thou hast no knowledge, then do not obey them; unto Me you shall return, and I shall tell you what you were doing.

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: And We have enjoined on man kindness unto parents. But if the twain strive to make thee associate with Me that of which thou hast no knowledge, obey them not. Unto Me is your return, and I shall declare unto you that which ye have been working.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: And We have enjoined on man to be good and dutiful to his parents, but if they strive to make you join with Me (in worship) anything (as a partner) of which you have no knowledge, then obey them not. Unto Me is your return, and I shall tell you what you used to do.

8.author: Itani
8.text: We have advised the human being to be good to his parents. But if they urge you to associate with Me something you have no knowledge of, do not obey them. To Me is your return; and I will inform you of what you used to do.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: We have enjoined upon man kindness to his parents, but if they exert pressure on you to associate with Me in My Divinity any that you do not know (to be My associate), do not obey them. To Me is your return, and I shall let you know all that you have done.

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: And We have enjoined on man to be dutiful to his parents; but if they strive to make you associate with Me, which you have no knowledge of, then obey them not. Unto Me is your return and I shall tell you what you used to do.

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: We have enjoined on man kindness to parents; but if they strive to make thee join with Me that of which thou hast no knowledge, then obey them not. Unto Me is your return and I shall tell you what ye used to do.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: We have enjoined man to be good to his parents. But if they urge you to ascribe to Me as partner that of which you have no knowledge, then do not obey them. To Me will be your return, whereat I will inform you concerning what you used to do.

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: We have charged the human to be kind to his parents. But if they bid you to associate with Me that which you have no knowledge, do not obey them. To Me you will return, and I shall inform you of all that you have done.

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: And We have enjoined upon man goodness to parents. But if they endeavor to make you associate with Me that of which you have no knowledge, do not obey them. To Me is your return, and I will inform you about what you used to do.

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: We have advised the human being, "Be kind to your parents. Do not obey them if they force you to consider equal to Me things which you do not know are such." You will all return (to Me) and I shall show all that you have done.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: And We have enjoined on man goodness to his parents, and if they contend with you that you should associate (others) with Me, of which you have no knowledge, do not obey them, to Me is your return, so I will inform you of what you did.

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: We have enjoined man to show kindness to his parents. But if they bid you associate with Me something about which you have no knowledge, do not obey them. To Me you shall all return, and I shall tell you about all that you have done.

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: We have enjoined on man kindness to parents: but if they (either of them) strive (to force) thee to join with Me (in worship) anything of which thou hast no knowledge, obey them not. Ye have (all) to return to me, and I will tell you (the truth) of all that ye did.

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: Wawassayna alinsana biwalidayhi husnan wain jahadaka litushrika bee ma laysa laka bihi AAilmun fala tutiAAhuma ilayya marjiAAukum faonabbiokum bima kuntum taAAmaloona

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: We shall admit those who believe and do the right among the righteous.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: And those who accepted faith and did good deeds – We shall indeed include them among the virtuous.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: And those who believe, and do righteous deeds assuredly We shall admit them among the righteous.

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: And those who believe and work righteous works--We shall surely cause them enter among the righteous.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: And for those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah and other items of Faith) and do righteous good deeds, surely, We shall make them enter in (the enterance of) the righteous (i.e. in Paradise).

9.author: Itani
9.text: Those who believe and do good works—We will admit them into the company of the righteous.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: As for those who believed and acted righteously, We shall certainly admit them among the righteous.

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: And for those who believe and do righteous deeds, surely, We shall admit them among the righteous.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: And as for those who believe and do good works, We verily shall make them enter in among the righteous.

9.author: Qarai
9.text: Those who have faith and do righteous deeds, We will surely admit them among the righteous.

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: Those who believe and do good works shall be admitted among the righteous.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: And those who believe and do righteous deeds - We will surely admit them among the righteous [into Paradise].

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: We shall admit the righteously striving believers into the company of the pious ones.

9.author: Shakir
9.text: And (as for) those who believe and do good, We will most surely cause them to enter among the good.

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: We shall surely admit those who believe and do good deeds to the company of the righteous.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: And those who believe and work righteous deeds,- them shall We admit to the company of the Righteous.

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Waallatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati lanudkhilannahum fee alssaliheena

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: There are among men those who say: "We believe in God;" yet if they happen to suffer in the cause of God they take oppression by men as punishment from God. And if help comes to them from your Lord, they say: "We were with you." Does not God know what is hidden in the hearts of men?

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: And some people say, “We believe in Allah” – so if they are afflicted with some adversity in Allah’s way, they consider the chaos created by men as the punishment from Allah; and if the help comes from your Lord, they will surely say, “Indeed we were with you”; does not Allah well know what is in the hearts of the entire creation?

10.author: Arberry
10.text: Some men there are who say, 'We believe in God,' but when such a man is hurt in God's cause, he makes the persecution of men as it were God's chastisement; then if help comes from thy Lord, he will say 'We were with you.' What, does not God know very well what is in the breasts of all beings?

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: Of mankind are some who say: we believe in Allah; then if they are afflicted in the way of Allah, they take the persecution of men even as the torment of Allah; and then, if succour cometh from thy Lord they say: verily we have been with you. Is not Allah the Best Knower of that which is in the breasts of the creatures?

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: Of mankind are some who say: "We believe in Allah," but if they are made to suffer for the sake of Allah, they consider the trial of mankind as Allah's punishment, and if victory comes from your Lord, (the hypocrites) will say: "Verily! We were with you (helping you)." Is not Allah Best Aware of what is in the breast of the 'Alamin (mankind and jinns).

10.author: Itani
10.text: Among the people is he who says, “We have believed in God.” Yet when he is harmed on God’s account, he equates the people's persecution with God’s retribution. And if help comes from your Lord, he says, “We were actually with you.” Is not God aware of what is inside the hearts of the people?

10.author: Maududi
10.text: Among people there are some who say: “We believe in Allah.” But when such a person is made to endure suffering in Allah's cause, he reckons the persecution he suffers at the hands of people as though it is a chastisement from Allah. But if victory comes from your Lord, the same person will say “We were with you.” Does Allah not know whatever is in the hearts of the people of the world?

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: Of mankind are some who say: "We believe in Allah." But if they are made to suffer for Allah, they consider the trial of mankind as Allah's punishment; and if victory comes from your Lord, they will say: "Verily, we were with you." Is not Allah Best Aware of what is in the breasts of the creatures

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: Of mankind is he who saith: We believe in Allah, but, if he be made to suffer for the sake of Allah, he mistaketh the persecution of mankind for Allah's punishment; and then, if victory cometh from thy Lord, will say: Lo! we were with you (all the while). Is not Allah Best Aware of what is in the bosoms of (His) creatures?

10.author: Qarai
10.text: Among the people there are those who say, ‘We have faith in Allah,’ but if such a one is tormented in Allah’s cause, he takes persecution by the people for Allah’s punishment. Yet if there comes any help from your Lord, they will say, ‘We were indeed with you.’ Does not Allah know best what is in the breasts of the creatures?

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: There are some people who say: 'We believe in Allah' yet when such is hurt in the cause of Allah, he makes the persecution of people as though it were the punishment of Allah. But then if help comes from his Lord, he will say: 'We were with you' Does Allah not know what is in the (people's) chests of the worlds?

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: And of the people are some who say, "We believe in Allah," but when one [of them] is harmed for [the cause of] Allah, they consider the trial of the people as [if it were] the punishment of Allah. But if victory comes from your Lord, they say, "Indeed, We were with you." Is not Allah most knowing of what is within the breasts of all creatures?

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: Some people say, "We have faith in God." But when they face some hardship for His cause, they begin to consider the persecution that they have experienced from people as a torment from God. When your Lord grants you a victory, they say, "We were with you." Does God not know best what is in the hearts of every creature?

10.author: Shakir
10.text: And among men is he who says: We believe in Allah; but when he is persecuted in (the way of) Allah he thinks the persecution of men to be as the chastisement of Allah; and if there come assistance from your Lord, they would most certainly say: Surely we were with you. What! is not Allah the best knower of what is in the breasts of mankind.

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: Some profess to believe in God, but when they suffer for God's cause they mistake the persecution of man for the punishment of God. But when help comes to you from God, they will say, "We have always been with you." Is not God fully aware of what is in the hearts of all creatures?

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: Then there are among men such as say, "We believe in Allah"; but when they suffer affliction in (the cause of) Allah, they treat men's oppression as if it were the Wrath of Allah! And if help comes (to thee) from thy Lord, they are sure to say, "We have (always) been with you!" Does not Allah know best all that is in the hearts of all creation?

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: Wamina alnnasi man yaqoolu amanna biAllahi faitha oothiya fee Allahi jaAAala fitnata alnnasi kaAAathabi Allahi walain jaa nasrun min rabbika layaqoolunna inna kunna maAAakum awalaysa Allahu biaAAlama bima fee sudoori alAAalameena

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: God will surely know the believers and know the hypocrites.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: And indeed Allah will make known the believers, and indeed He will expose the hypocrites.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: God surely knows the believers, and He knows the hypocrites.

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: And surely Allah shall come to know those who believe, and surely He shall come to know the hypocrites.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: Verily, Allah knows those who believe, and verily, He knows the hypocrites [i.e. Allah will test the people with good and hard days to discriminate the good from the wicked (although Allah knows all that before putting them to test)].

11.author: Itani
11.text: God certainly knows those who believe, and He certainly knows the hypocrites.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: Allah will surely ascertain who are the believers and who are the hypocrites.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: And indeed Allah knows those who believe, and verily, He knows the hypocrites.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Verily Allah knoweth those who believe, and verily He knoweth the hypocrites.

11.author: Qarai
11.text: Allah shall surely ascertain those who have faith, and He shall surely ascertain the hypocrites.

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: Most certainly Allah knows the believers and the hypocrites.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: And Allah will surely make evident those who believe, and He will surely make evident the hypocrites.

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: God certainly knows all about the believers and the hypocrites.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: And most certainly Allah will know those who believe and most certainly He will know the hypocrites.

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: Most certainly God will mark out those who believe and mark out those who are hypocrites.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: And Allah most certainly knows those who believe, and as certainly those who are Hypocrites.

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: WalayaAAlamanna Allahu allatheena amanoo walayaAAlamanna almunafiqeena

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: Those who deny say to those who affirm: "Follow our way; we shall carry the burden of your sins." But they cannot carry the burden of their sins in the least. They are liars indeed.

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: And the disbelievers said to the Muslims, “Follow our path and we will bear your sins”; whereas they will not bear anything from their sins; they are indeed liars.

12.author: Arberry
12.text: The unbelievers say to the believers, 'Follow our path, and let us carry your offences'; yet they cannot carry anything, even of their own offences; they are truly liars.

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: And those who disbelieve say unto those who believe: follow our way, and we shall surely bear your sins; whereas they shall not bear aught of their sins; verily they are the liars.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: And those who disbelieve say to those who believe: "Follow our way and we will verily bear your sins," never will they bear anything of their sins. Surely, they are liars.

12.author: Itani
12.text: Those who disbelieve say to those who believe, “Follow our way, and we will carry your sins.” In no way can they carry any of their sins. They are liars.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: The unbelievers say to the believers: “Follow our way and we will carry the burden of your sins.” (They say so even though) they are not going to carry any part of their sins. Surely they are lying.

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: And those who disbelieve say to those who believe: "Follow our way and let us bear your sins." Never will they bear anything of their sins. Surely, they are liars.

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: Those who disbelieve say unto those who believe: Follow our way (of religion) and we verily will bear your sins (for you). They cannot bear aught of their sins. Lo! they verily are liars.

12.author: Qarai
12.text: The faithless say to the faithful, ‘Follow our way and we will bear [responsibility for] your iniquities.’ They will not bear anything of their iniquities. They are indeed liars.

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: The unbelievers say to those who believe: 'Follow our path, and we will carry your sins' Yet they cannot carry even a thing of their sins. They are surely lying.

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: And those who disbelieve say to those who believe, "Follow our way, and we will carry your sins." But they will not carry anything of their sins. Indeed, they are liars.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: The unbelievers say to the believers. "Follow our way. We shall take the responsibility for your sins." They cannot take responsibility for any of the sins. They are only liars.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: And those who disbelieve say to those who believe: Follow our path and we will bear your wrongs. And never shall they be the bearers of any of their wrongs; most surely they are liars.

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: Those who deny the truth say to the faithful, "Follow our way, and we will bear the burden of your sins." But they will bear none of their sins. They are surely lying.

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: And the Unbelievers say to those who believe: "Follow our path, and we will bear (the consequences) of your faults." Never in the least will they bear their faults: in fact they are liars!

12.author: Transliteration
12.text: Waqala allatheena kafaroo lillatheena amanoo ittabiAAoo sabeelana walnahmil khatayakum wama hum bihamileena min khatayahum min shayin innahum lakathiboona

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: They will carry their own loads and other loads besides their own; and will surely be questioned on the Day of Resurrection about what they contrived.

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: And they will surely bear their own burdens and some other burdens along with their own; and they will undoubtedly be questioned on the Day of Resurrection concerning what they had fabricated.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: They shall certainly carry their loads, and other loads along with their loads, and upon the Day of Resurrection they shall surely be questioned concerning that they were forging.

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And surely they shall bear their loads and other loads beside their own loads, and surely they shall be questioned on the Day of Resurrection concerning that which they were wont to fabricate.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: And verily, they shall bear their own loads, and other loads besides their own, and verily, they shall be questioned on the Day of Resurrection about that which they used to fabricate.

13.author: Itani
13.text: They will carry their own loads, and other loads with their own. And they will be questioned on the Day of Resurrection concerning what they used to fabricate.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: They will certainly carry their own burdens and other burdens besides their own. They will assuredly be called to account on the Day of Resurrection concerning the fabrications which they contrived.

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: And verily, they shall bear their own loads, and other loads besides their own; and verily, they shall be questioned on the Day of Resurrection about that which they used to fabricate.

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: But they verily will bear their own loads and other loads beside their own, and they verily will be questioned on the Day of Resurrection concerning that which they invented.

13.author: Qarai
13.text: But they will carry their own burdens and other burdens along with their own burdens, and they will surely be questioned on the Day of Resurrection concerning that which they used to fabricate.

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: They shall certainly carry their loads, and other loads besides their loads, and on the Day of Resurrection, they shall be questioned about what they forged.

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: But they will surely carry their [own] burdens and [other] burdens along with their burdens, and they will surely be questioned on the Day of Resurrection about what they used to invent.

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: Besides the other burdens that they will have to carry, they will certainly be loaded with the burden of their own sins. They will be questioned on the Day of Judgment about what they had falsely invented.

13.author: Shakir
13.text: And most certainly they shall carry their own burdens, and other burdens with their own burdens, and most certainly they shall be questioned on the resurrection day as to what they forged.

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: They shall bear their own burdens, and other burdens besides. On the Day of Resurrection they shall be questioned about their false assertions.

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: They will bear their own burdens, and (other) burdens along with their own, and on the Day of Judgments they will be called to account for their falsehoods.

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: Walayahmilunna athqalahum waathqalan maAAa athqalihim walayusalunna yawma alqiyamati AAamma kanoo yaftaroona

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: We sent Noah to his people, and he lived with them a thousand years minus fifty. Then they were caught by the deluge for they were evil.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: And indeed We sent Nooh to his people – he therefore stayed with them for a thousand years, less fifty; so the flood seized them, and they were unjust.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: Indeed, We sent Noah to his people, and he tarried among them a thousand years, all but fifty; so the Flood seized them, while they were evildoers.

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And assuredly We sent Nuh unto his people. Then he tarried among them for a thousand years, save tifty years; and then deluge overtook them, while they were wrong-doers.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: And indeed We sent Nuh (Noah) to his people, and he stayed among them a thousand years less fifty years [inviting them to believe in the Oneness of Allah (Monotheism), and discard the false gods and other deities], and the Deluge overtook them while they were Zalimun (wrong-doers, polytheists, disbelievers, etc.).

14.author: Itani
14.text: We sent Noah to his people, and He stayed among them for a thousand years minus fifty years. Then the Deluge swept them; for they were wrongdoers.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: We did indeed send Noah to his people and he lived among them a thousand years save fifty. Eventually the Flood overtook them while they were engaged in wrongdoing.

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: And indeed We sent Nuh to his people, and he stayed among them a thousand years less fifty years; so the Deluge overtook them while they were wrongdoers.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: And verily we sent Noah (as Our messenger) unto his folk, and he continued with them for a thousand years save fifty years; and the flood engulfed them, for they were wrong-doers.

14.author: Qarai
14.text: Certainly We sent Noah to his people, and he remained with them for a thousand-less-fifty years. Then the flood overtook them while they were wrongdoers.

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: Indeed, We sent Noah to his nation, and he lived amongst them for a thousand years, less fifty (but they belied him), then the Flood seized them while they were harmdoers.

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And We certainly sent Noah to his people, and he remained among them a thousand years minus fifty years, and the flood seized them while they were wrongdoers.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: We sent Noah to his people and he lived with them for nine hundred and fifty years, then the flood engulfed them for their injustice.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And certainly We sent Nuh to his people, so he remained among them a thousand years save fifty years. And the deluge overtook them, while they were unjust.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: We sent Noah to his people and he lived among them for fifty short of a thousand years; then the deluge overtook them, for they were wrongdoers.

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: We (once) sent Noah to his people, and he tarried among them a thousand years less fifty: but the Deluge overwhelmed them while they (persisted in) sin.

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Walaqad arsalna noohan ila qawmihi falabitha feehim alfa sanatin illa khamseena AAaman faakhathahumu alttoofanu wahum thalimoona

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: But We saved him and those with him in the ark, and made it a sign for the creatures of the world.

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: And We rescued him and the people aboard the ship, and made the ship a sign for the entire world.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: Yet We delivered him, and those who were in the ship, and appointed it for a sign unto all beings.

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: Then We delivered him and those in the ark, and made it a sign unto the worlds.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: Then We saved him and those with him in the ship, and made it (the ship) as an Ayah (a lesson, a warning, etc.) for the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

15.author: Itani
15.text: But We saved him, together with the company of the Ark, and We made it a sign for all peoples.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: Then We rescued Noah together with the people in the Ark and made it (that is, the Ark) a lesson for all people.

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: Then We saved him and the Companions of the Boat, and made it an Ayah for all people.

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: And We rescued him and those with him in the ship, and made of it a portent for the peoples.

15.author: Qarai
15.text: Then We delivered him and those who were in the Ark, and made it a sign for all the nations.

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: But We saved him and all who were in the Ark, and made (the event) a sign to the worlds.

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: But We saved him and the companions of the ship, and We made it a sign for the worlds.

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: We saved Noah and the people in the Ark and made (their case) a miracle for the world.

15.author: Shakir
15.text: So We delivered him and the inmates of the ark, and made it a sign to the nations.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: But We saved him and those who were with him in the Ark and made the event a sign for mankind.

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: But We saved him and the companions of the Ark, and We made the (Ark) a Sign for all peoples!

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Faanjaynahu waashaba alssafeenati wajaAAalnaha ayatan lilAAalameena

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: And (remember) Abraham who said to his people: "Worship God and be obedient to Him. This is better for you if you understand.

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: And (remember) Ibrahim – when he said to his people, “Worship Allah and fear Him; that is better for you, if you knew!”

16.author: Arberry
16.text: And Abraham, when he said to his people, 'Serve God, and fear Him; that is better for you, did you know.

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: And We sent Ibrahim. Recall what time he said unto his people: worship Allah, and fear Him; that is best for you if ye but knew.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: And (remember) Ibrahim (Abraham) when he said to his people: "Worship Allah (Alone), and fear Him, that is better for you if you did but know.

16.author: Itani
16.text: And Abraham, when he said to his people, “Worship God, and fear Him. That is better for you, if you only knew.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: We sent Abraham and he said to his people: “Serve Allah and fear Him. This is better for you if you only knew.

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: And (remember) Ibrahim when he said to his people: "Worship Allah, and have Taqwa of Him, that is better for you if you know."

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: And Abraham! (Remember) when he said unto his folk: Serve Allah, and keep your duty unto Him; that is better for you if ye did but know.

16.author: Qarai
16.text: And Abraham, when he said to his people, ‘Worship Allah and be wary of Him. That is better for you, should you know.

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: And (remember) Abraham. When he said to his people 'Worship Allah and fear Him. That would be best for you, if you but knew.

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: And [We sent] Abraham, when he said to his people, "Worship Allah and fear Him. That is best for you, if you should know.

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: Abraham told his people, "Worship God and have fear of Him. It is better for you if only you knew it.

16.author: Shakir
16.text: And (We sent) Ibrahim, when he said to his people: Serve Allah and be careful of (your duty to) Him; this is best for you, if you did but know:

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: Tell of Abraham. He said to his people, "Worship God and fear Him, that would be best for you, if only you knew.

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: And (We also saved) Abraham: behold, he said to his people, "Serve Allah and fear Him: that will be best for you- If ye understand!

16.author: Transliteration
16.text: Waibraheema ith qala liqawmihi oAAbudoo Allaha waittaqoohu thalikum khayrun lakum in kuntum taAAlamoona

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: You worship idols in place of God and invent lies. Surely those you worship other than God have no power over your means of livelihood. So seek your sustenance from God, and worship Him and give Him thanks. To Him will you be brought back in the end.

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: “You worship only idols instead of Allah, and only forge a clear lie; indeed those whom you worship instead of Allah do not control your sustenance – therefore seek your sustenance from Allah, and worship Him, and be grateful to Him; it is towards Him that you will be returned.”

17.author: Arberry
17.text: You only serve, apart from God, idols and you create a calumny; those you serve, apart from God, have no power to provide for you. So seek after your provision with God, and serve Him, and be thankful to Him; unto Him you shall be returned.

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Ye only worship images instead of Allah, and ye create a falsehood. Verily those whom ye worship instead of Allah own no provision for you. Wherefore seek with Allah provision, and worship Him, and give thanks unto Him: unto Him ye shall be returned.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: "You worship besides Allah only idols, and you only invent falsehood. Verily, those whom you worship besides Allah have no power to give you provision, so seek your provision from Allah (Alone), and worship Him (Alone), and be grateful to Him. To Him (Alone) you will be brought back.

17.author: Itani
17.text: You worship idols besides God, and you fabricate falsehoods. Those you worship, instead of God, cannot provide you with livelihood. So seek your livelihood from God, and worship Him, and thank Him. To Him you will be returned.”

17.author: Maududi
17.text: Those that you worship instead of Allah are merely idols, and you are simply inventing lies (about them). Indeed those whom you worship beside Allah have no power to provide you with any sustenance. So seek your sustenance from Allah and serve only Him and give thanks to Him alone. It is to Him that you will be sent back.

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: You worship besides Allah only idols, and you only invent falsehood. Verily, those whom you worship besides Allah have no power to give you provision, so seek from Allah your provision, and worship Him, and be grateful to Him. To Him you will be brought back.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: Ye serve instead of Allah only idols, and ye only invent a lie. Lo! those whom ye serve instead of Allah own no provision for you. So seek your provision from Allah, and serve Him, and give thanks unto Him, (for) unto Him ye will be brought back.

17.author: Qarai
17.text: What you worship instead of Allah are mere idols, and you invent a lie. Indeed those whom you worship besides Allah have no control over your provision. So seek all [your] provision from Allah, and worship Him and thank Him, and to Him you shall be brought back.’

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: You only worship, other than Allah, idols and invent falsehoods. Those whom you worship, other than Allah, have no power to give you provision. Therefore seek the provision of Allah, and worship Him. Give thanks to Him, to Him you shall return'

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: You only worship, besides Allah, idols, and you produce a falsehood. Indeed, those you worship besides Allah do not possess for you [the power of] provision. So seek from Allah provision and worship Him and be grateful to Him. To Him you will be returned."

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: You worship idols besides God and you create falsehood. Whatever you worship besides Him cannot provide you with anything for your sustenance. Seek your sustenance from the bounties of God. Worship Him. Give Him thanks. To Him you will all return."

17.author: Shakir
17.text: You only worship idols besides Allah and you create a lie surely they whom you serve besides Allah do not control for you any sustenance, therefore seek the sustenance from Allah and serve Him and be grateful to Him; to Him you shall be brought back.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: You worship idols besides God and fabricate falsehoods. Those whom you worship besides God have no power to provide sustenance for you. So seek your sustenance from God and worship Him and be grateful to Him, for to Him you shall return.

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: "For ye do worship idols besides Allah, and ye invent falsehood. The things that ye worship besides Allah have no power to give you sustenance: then seek ye sustenance from Allah, serve Him, and be grateful to Him: to Him will be your return.

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Innama taAAbudoona min dooni Allahi awthanan watakhluqoona ifkan inna allatheena taAAbudoona min dooni Allahi la yamlikoona lakum rizqan faibtaghoo AAinda Allahi alrrizqa waoAAbudoohu waoshkuroo lahu ilayhi turjaAAoona

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: But if you deny, then many a people have denied before you. The duty of the apostle is to convey the message clearly."

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: “And if you deny, then many nations have denied before you; and the Noble Messenger is not responsible except to plainly convey (the message).”

18.author: Arberry
18.text: 'But if you cry me lies, nations cried lies before you; and it is only for the Messenger to deliver the Manifest Message.'

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: And if ye belie me, then communities before you have belied: and upon the apostle is naught but a preaching manifest.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: "And if you deny, then nations before you have denied (their Messengers). And the duty of the Messenger is only to convey (the Message) plainly."

18.author: Itani
18.text: If you disbelieve, communities before you have also disbelieved. The Messenger is only responsible for clear transmission.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: And if you give the lie (to the Messenger), then many nations before you also gave the lie (to their Messengers). The Messenger is charged with no other duty than to deliver the Message in clear terms.”

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: And if you deny, then nations before you have denied. And the duty of the Messenger is only to convey plainly."

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: But if ye deny, then nations have denied before you. The messenger is only to convey (the message) plainly.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: If you impugn [the Apostle’s teaching], then [other] nations have impugned [likewise] before you, and the Apostle’s duty is only to communicate in clear terms.

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: If you belie me, other nations before you also belied. It is only for a Messenger to deliver the clear deliverance.

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: And if you [people] deny [the message] - already nations before you have denied. And there is not upon the Messenger except [the duty of] clear notification.

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: If you, (pagans), call our (revelations) lies, certainly many generations living before you have also done the same thing. The duty of a Messenger is only to preach clearly.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: And if you reject (the truth), nations before you did indeed reject (the truth); and nothing is incumbent on the apostle but a plain delivering (of the message).

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: If you reject the truth, other communities before you also rejected the truth. The messenger's responsibility is only to convey the message clearly."

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: "And if ye reject (the Message), so did generations before you: and the duty of the messenger is only to preach publicly (and clearly)."

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Wain tukaththiboo faqad kaththaba omamun min qablikum wama AAala alrrasooli illa albalaghu almubeenu

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: Do they not see how God originates creation, then reverts it back? This is indeed how inevitably the law of God works;

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: Have they not seen how Allah initiates the creation, then will create it again? Indeed that is easy for Allah.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: (Have they not seen how God originates creation, then brings it back again? Surely that is an easy matter for God.

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: Observe they not what wise Allah originateth creation? And then He shall restore it. Verily for Allah that is easy.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: See they not how Allah originates creation, then repeats it. Verily, that is easy for Allah.

19.author: Itani
19.text: Have they not seen how God originates the creation, and then reproduces it? This is easy for God.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: Have they never observed how Allah creates for the first time and then repeats it? Indeed (to repeat the creation of a thing) is even easier for Allah (than creating it for the first time).

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: See they not how Allah originates the creation, then repeats it. Verily, that is easy for Allah.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: See they not how Allah produceth creation, then reproduceth it? Lo! for Allah that is easy.

19.author: Qarai
19.text: Have they not regarded how Allah originates the creation? Then He will bring it back. That is indeed easy for Allah.

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: Do they not see how Allah originates the Creation, and then brings it back? That is an easy matter for Allah.

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: Have they not considered how Allah begins creation and then repeats it? Indeed that, for Allah, is easy.

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: Have they not seen how God begins the creation and then turns it back? This is not difficult at all for God.

19.author: Shakir
19.text: What! do they not consider how Allah originates the creation, then reproduces it? Surely that is easy to Allah.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: Do they not see how God originates creation, then reproduces it? That surely is easy for God.

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: See they not how Allah originates creation, then repeats it: truly that is easy for Allah.

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Awalam yaraw kayfa yubdio Allahu alkhalqa thumma yuAAeeduhu inna thalika AAala Allahi yaseerun

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: Say: "Travel on the earth and see how He originated creation. Then (you will know) how God will raise the last raising (of the dead). Surely God has power over everything,

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “Travel in the land and see how He creates for the first time, then Allah brings forth the next development; indeed Allah is Able to do all things.”

20.author: Arberry
20.text: Say: Journey in the land, then behold how He originated creation; then God causes the second growth to grow; God is powerful over everything,

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: Say thou: go about in the land and behold what wise He hath originated creation; and then Allah shall produce another production; verily Allah is over everything Potent.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: Say: "Travel in the land and see how (Allah) originated creation, and then Allah will bring forth (resurrect) the creation of the Hereafter (i.e. resurrection after death). Verily, Allah is Able to do all things."

20.author: Itani
20.text: Say, “Roam the earth, and observe how He originated the creation.” Then God will bring about the next existence. God has power over all things.”

20.author: Maududi
20.text: Say: “Go about the earth and see how He created for the first time, and then Allah will recreate life.” Surely, Allah has power over everything.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: Say: "Travel in the land and see how He originated the creation, and then Allah will bring forth the creation of the Hereafter. Verily, Allah is able to do all things."

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: Say (O Muhammad): Travel in the land and see how He originated creation, then Allah bringeth forth the later growth. Lo! Allah is Able to do all things.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: Say, ‘Travel over the land and observe how He has originated the creation.’ Then Allah will bring about the genesis of the Hereafter. Indeed Allah has power over all things.

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: Say: 'Travel in the land and see how He started the Creation. Then Allah will originate the Everlasting Life. Allah has power over all things.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: Say, [O Muhammad], "Travel through the land and observe how He began creation. Then Allah will produce the final creation. Indeed Allah, over all things, is competent."

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: (Muhammad), say to them, "Travel through the land and see how He has begun the creation and how He will invent the next life. God has power over all things.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: Say: Travel in the earth and see how He makes the first creation, then Allah creates the latter creation; surely Allah has power over all things.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: Tell them, "Roam the earth and see how He originated creation. Then God will bring into being your second life. God has power over all things.

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: Say: "Travel through the earth and see how Allah did originate creation; so will Allah produce a later creation: for Allah has power over all things.

20.author: Transliteration
20.text: Qul seeroo fee alardi faonthuroo kayfa badaa alkhalqa thumma Allahu yunshio alnnashata alakhirata inna Allaha AAala kulli shayin qadeerun

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: Punish whom He will, and have mercy on whom He please. And to Him will you be brought back in the end.

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: He punishes whomever He wills and has mercy upon whomever He wills; and towards Him only you are to return.

21.author: Arberry
21.text: chastising whom He will, and having mercy on whomsoever He will, and unto Him you shall be turned.

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: He shall torment whomsoever He Will, and shall show mercy unto whomsoever He Will, and unto Him ye shall be returned.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: He punishes whom He will, and shows mercy to whom He will, and to Him you will be returned.

21.author: Itani
21.text: He punishes whom He wills, and He grants mercy to whom He wills, and to Him you will be restored.

21.author: Maududi
21.text: He chastises whom He will and forgives whom He will. To Him all of you will be sent back.

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: He punishes whom He wills, and shows mercy to whom He wills; and to Him you will be returned.

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: He punisheth whom He will and showeth mercy unto whom He will, and unto Him ye will be turned.

21.author: Qarai
21.text: He will punish whomever He wishes and have mercy on whomever He wishes, and to Him you will be returned.

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: He punishes whom He will and has mercy to whom He will. To Him you shall be turned.

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: He punishes whom He wills and has mercy upon whom He wills, and to Him you will be returned.

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: He punishes or grants mercy to whomever He wants and to Him you will all return.

21.author: Shakir
21.text: He punishes whom He pleases and has mercy on whom He pleases, and to Him you shall be turned back.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: He punishes whom He will and shows mercy to whom He pleases. And to Him you shall be returned.

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: "He punishes whom He pleases, and He grants Mercy to whom He pleases, and towards Him are ye turned.

21.author: Transliteration
21.text: YuAAaththibu man yashao wayarhamu man yashao wailayhi tuqlaboona

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: Escape Him you cannot either in the earth or in the sky; and you have no friend or helper apart from God.

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: Neither can you escape (from His control) in the earth nor in the sky; and for you, other than Allah, there is neither a friend nor a supporter.

22.author: Arberry
22.text: You are not able to frustrate Him either in the earth or in heaven; and you have not, apart from God, either protector or helper.

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: And ye cannot escape in the earth nor in the heaven, and beside Allah there is for you no friend nor helper.

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: And you cannot escape in the earth or in the heaven. And besides Allah you have neither any Wali (Protector or Guardian) nor any Helper.

22.author: Itani
22.text: You cannot escape, on earth or in the heaven; and you have no protector and no savior besides God.

22.author: Maududi
22.text: You cannot overpower Allah, neither on the earth nor in the heaven. None can protect you from Allah nor come to your aid against Him.

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: And you cannot escape in the earth or in the heaven. And besides Allah you have neither any protector nor any helper.

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: Ye cannot escape (from Him) in the earth or in the sky, and beside Allah there is for you no friend or helper.

22.author: Qarai
22.text: You cannot frustrate Him on the earth or in the sky, nor do you have besides Allah any guardian or helper.

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: You are not able to frustrate Him either in the earth or heaven, nor have you, other than Allah, a guardian or helper.

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: And you will not cause failure [to Allah] upon the earth or in the heaven. And you have not other than Allah any protector or any helper.

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: You cannot challenge God in the heavens or in the earth. No one besides God is your guardian or helper.

22.author: Shakir
22.text: And you shall not escape in the earth nor in the heaven, and you have neither a protector nor a helper besides Allah.

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: You cannot defeat His purpose on earth or in heaven; nor have you any friend or helper besides God."

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: "Not on earth nor in heaven will ye be able (fleeing) to frustrate (his Plan), nor have ye, besides Allah, any protector or helper."

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Wama antum bimuAAjizeena fee alardi wala fee alssamai wama lakum min dooni Allahi min waliyyin wala naseerin

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: Those who deny the signs of God and the meeting with Him, cannot have hope of My mercy. There is a painful punishment for them.

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: And those who did not believe in My signs and in meeting Me, are those who have no hope of My mercy, and for them is a painful punishment.

23.author: Arberry
23.text: And those who disbelieve in God's signs and the encounter with Him -- they despair of My mercy, and there awaits them a painful chastisement.)

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: Those who disbelieve in the signs of Allah and in their meeting with Him, they shall despair of My mercy, and they! theirs shall be a torment afflictive.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: And those who disbelieve in the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of Allah and the Meeting with Him, it is they who have no hope of My Mercy, and it is they who will (have) a painful torment.

23.author: Itani
23.text: Those who disbelieved in God’s signs and His encounter—these have despaired of My mercy. For them is a painful torment.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: Those who disbelieved in Allah's signs and in meeting Him, it is they who have despaired of My Mercy; it is they for whom a painful chastisement lies ahead.

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: And those who disbelieve in the Ayat of Allah and meeting with Him, such have no hope of My mercy: and for such there is a painful torment.

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: Those who disbelieve in the revelations of Allah and in (their) Meeting with Him, such have no hope of My mercy. For such there is a painful doom.

23.author: Qarai
23.text: Those who deny the signs of Allah and the encounter with Him—they have despaired of My mercy, and for such there is a painful punishment.

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: Those who disbelieve the verses of Allah and the meeting with Him shall despair of My Mercy, for them there awaits a painful punishment.

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: And the ones who disbelieve in the signs of Allah and the meeting with Him - those have despaired of My mercy, and they will have a painful punishment.

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: Those who have rejected God's revelations have no hope in receiving His mercy. They will face a painful torment.

23.author: Shakir
23.text: And (as to) those who disbelieve in the communications of Allah and His meeting, they have despaired of My mercy, and these it is that shall have a painful punishment.

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: Those who deny God's revelations and the meeting with Him -- it is they who have despaired of My mercy. They will suffer a grievous punishment.

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: Those who reject the Signs of Allah and the Meeting with Him (in the Hereafter),- it is they who shall despair of My Mercy: it is they who will (suffer) a most grievous Penalty.

23.author: Transliteration
23.text: Waallatheena kafaroo biayati Allahi waliqaihi olaika yaisoo min rahmatee waolaika lahum AAathabun aleemun

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: The people (of Abraham) had no answer except: "Kill him or burn him;" but God saved him from the fire. There are lessons in this for those who believe.

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: So his people could not answer him except to say, “Kill him or burn him” – so Allah rescued him from the fire; indeed in this are signs for people who believe.

24.author: Arberry
24.text: But the only answer of his people was that they said, 'Slay him, or burn him!' Then God delivered him from the fire; surely in that are signs for a people who believe.

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: Then the answer of his people was naught but that they said: slay him, or burn him. Then Allah delivered him from the fire; verily herein are signs for a people who believe.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: So nothing was the answer of [Ibrahim's (Abraham)] people except that they said: "Kill him or burn him." Then Allah saved him from the fire. Verily, in this are indeed signs for a people who believe.

24.author: Itani
24.text: But the only response from his people was their saying, “Kill him, or burn him.” But God saved him from the fire. Surely in that are signs for people who believe.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: The people (of Abraham) had no other answer than to say: “Kill him or burn him.” But Allah delivered him from the fire. There are many Signs in this for those who believe.

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: So nothing was the answer of people except that they said: "Kill him or burn him." Then Allah saved him from the fire. Verily, in this are indeed signs for a people who believe.

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: But the answer of his folk was only that they said: "Kill him" or "Burn him." Then Allah saved him from the Fire. Lo! herein verily are portents for folk who believe.

24.author: Qarai
24.text: But the only answer of his people was that they said, ‘Kill him, or burn him.’ Then Allah delivered him from the fire. There are indeed signs in that for a people who have faith.

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: The only answer his nation gave was that they said: 'Kill him, or burn him! 'But Allah saved him from the fire. Surely, in this there are signs for a nation who believe.

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: And the answer of Abraham's people was not but that they said, "Kill him or burn him," but Allah saved him from the fire. Indeed in that are signs for a people who believe.

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: (Abraham's) people had no answer except suggesting, "Kill him or burn him." But God saved him from the fire. In this there is evidence (of truth) for the believing people.

24.author: Shakir
24.text: So naught was the answer of his people except that they said: Slay him or burn him; then Allah delivered him from the fire; most surely there are signs in this for a people who believe.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: The only response of Abraham's people was, "Kill him or burn him!" But God saved him from the fire. Surely in this there are signs for people who believe.

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: So naught was the answer of (Abraham's) people except that they said: "Slay him or burn him." But Allah did save him from the Fire. Verily in this are Signs for people who believe.

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Fama kana jawaba qawmihi illa an qaloo oqtuloohu aw harriqoohu faanjahu Allahu mina alnnari inna fee thalika laayatin liqawmin yuminoona

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: He said: "You have taken to idols through mutual affection in this life, in place of God, but on the Day of Resurrection you will disown and curse each other, and your abode will be Hell, and you will have none to help you."

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: And Ibrahim said, “You have chosen only idols instead of Allah, with whom your friendship is only in the life of this world; then on the Day of Resurrection you will deny and curse each other; and the destination for all of you is hell, and you do not have supporters.”

25.author: Arberry
25.text: And he said, 'You have only taken to yourselves idols, apart from God, as a mark of mutual love between you in the present life; then upon the Day of Resurrection you will deny one another, and you will curse one another, and your refuge will be the Fire, and you will have no helpers.'

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: And he said: ye have taken images instead of Allah out of affection between you in the life of the world; but on the Day of Resurrection ye shall deny each other and ye shall curse each other, and your resort shall be the Fire, and ye shall have no helpers.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: And [Ibrahim (Abraham)] said: "You have taken (for worship) idols instead of Allah, and the love between you is only in the life of this world, but on the Day of Resurrection, you shall disown each other, and curse each other, and your abode will be the Fire, and you shall have no helper."

25.author: Itani
25.text: And he said, “You have chosen idols instead of God, out of affection for one another in the worldly life. But then, on the Day of Resurrection, you will disown one another, and curse one another. Your destiny is Hell, and you will have no saviors.”

25.author: Maududi
25.text: He said: “You have taken up idols instead of Allah as a bond of love among yourselves in the present life, but on the Day of Resurrection you will disown and curse one another. Your refuge shall be the Fire, and none will come to your aid.”

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: And (Ibrahim) said: "You have taken idols instead of Allah. The love between you is only in the life of this world, but on the Day of Resurrection, you shall deny each other, and curse each other, and your abode will be the Fire, and you shall have no helper."

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: He said: Ye have chosen only idols instead of Allah. The love between you is only in the life of the world. Then on the Day of Resurrection ye will deny each other and curse each other, and your abode will be the Fire, and ye will have no helpers.

25.author: Qarai
25.text: He said, ‘You have taken idols [for worship] besides Allah for the sake of [mutual] affection amongst yourselves in the life of the world. Then on the Day of Resurrection you will disown one another and curse one another, and the Fire will be your abode, and you will not have any helpers.’

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: He said: 'Other than Allah, you have taken to yourselves idols as an affection between yourselves in the present life. Then, on the Day of Resurrection, you shall deny one another and curse one another, and your refuge will be the Fire, and none shall help you'

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: And [Abraham] said, "You have only taken, other than Allah, idols as [a bond of] affection among you in worldly life. Then on the Day of Resurrection you will deny one another and curse one another, and your refuge will be the Fire, and you will not have any helpers."

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: Abraham said, "You believe in idols besides God only out of worldly love, but on the Day of Judgment you will reject and condemn each other. Your dwelling will be fire and no one will help you."

25.author: Shakir
25.text: And he said: You have only taken for yourselves idols besides Allah by way of friendship between you in this world's life, then on the resurrection day some of you shall deny others, and some of you shall curse others, and your abode is the fire, and you shall not have any helpers.

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: Abraham said, "You have taken up the worship of idols, instead of God, to promote friendship between yourselves in the present life. But on the Day of Judgement, you will disown and curse one another. Your abode will be the Fire and you will have no helpers."

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: And he said: "For you, ye have taken (for worship) idols besides Allah, out of mutual love and regard between yourselves in this life; but on the Day of Judgment ye shall disown each other and curse each other: and your abode will be the Fire, and ye shall have none to help."

25.author: Transliteration
25.text: Waqala innama ittakhathtum min dooni Allahi awthanan mawaddata baynikum fee alhayati alddunya thumma yawma alqiyamati yakfuru baAAdukum bibaAAdin wayalAAanu baAAdukum baAAdan wamawakumu alnnaru wama lakum min nasireena

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: Then Lot believed in him; and (Abraham) said: "I will separate myself and take refuge in my Lord. Surely He is all-mighty and all-wise."

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: So Lut believed in him; and Ibrahim said, “I migrate towards my Lord; indeed only He is the Almighty, the Wise.”

26.author: Arberry
26.text: But Lot believed him; and he said, 'I will flee to my Lord; He is the All-mighty, the All-wise.'

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: And Lut believed in him. And he said: verily I am going to migrate to my Lord; verily He is the Mighty, the Wise.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: So Lout (Lot) believed in him [Ibrahim's (Abraham) Message of Islamic Monotheism]. He [Ibrahim (Abraham)] said: "I will emigrate for the sake of my Lord. Verily, He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise."

26.author: Itani
26.text: Then Lot believed in him, and said, “I am emigrating to my Lord. He is the Noble, the Wise.”

26.author: Maududi
26.text: Then did Lot believe him, and Abraham said: “I am emigrating unto my Lord. He is All-Powerful, All-Wise,”

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: So, Lut believed in him. He said: "I will emigrate for the sake of my Lord. Verily, He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise."

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: And Lot believed him, and said: Lo! I am a fugitive unto my Lord. Lo! He, only He, is the Mighty, the Wise.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: Thereupon Lot believed in him, and he said, ‘Indeed I am migrating toward my Lord. Indeed He is the All-mighty, the All-wise.’

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: Lot believed him, and said: 'I will migrate to my Lord; He is the Almighty, the Wise'

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: And Lot believed him. [Abraham] said, "Indeed, I will emigrate to [the service of] my Lord. Indeed, He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise."

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: Only Lot believed in (Abraham) and said, "I seek refuge in my Lord, for He is Majestic and All-wise".

26.author: Shakir
26.text: And Lut believed in Him, and he said: I am fleeing to my Lord, surely He is the Mighty, the Wise.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: Lot was the one who believed in him and said, "I shall migrate to another land for the sake of my Lord. He is the Mighty One, the Wise One."

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: But Lut had faith in Him: He said: "I will leave home for the sake of my Lord: for He is Exalted in Might, and Wise."

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Faamana lahu lootun waqala innee muhajirun ila rabbee innahu huwa alAAazeezu alhakeemu

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: So We bestowed on him Isaac and Jacob and gave his progeny prophethood and scripture, and rewarded him in this world, and in the next he will be among the upright.

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: And We bestowed Ishaq and Yaqub to him, and kept the Prophethood and the Book among his descendants, and We gave him his reward in the world; and in the Hereafter he is indeed among those who deserve Our proximity.

27.author: Arberry
27.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and We appointed the Prophecy and the Book to be among his seed; We gave him his wage in this world, and in the world to come he shall be among the righteous.

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: And We bestowed on him Is-haq and Y'aqub, and We placed among his posterity prophethood and the Book, and We vouchsafed unto him his hire in the World, and verily in the Hereafter he shall be of the righteous.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: And We bestowed on him [Ibrahim (Abraham)], Ishaque (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob), and ordained among his offspring Prophethood and the Book [i.e. the Taurat (Torah) (to Musa - Moses), the Injeel (Gospel) (to 'Iesa - Jesus), the Quran (to Muhammad SAW), all from the offspring of Ibrahim (Abraham)], and We granted him his reward in this world, and verily, in the Hereafter he is indeed among the righteous.

27.author: Itani
27.text: And We granted him Isaac and Jacob, and conferred on his descendants the Prophethood and the Book, and gave him his reward in this life; and in the Hereafter he will be among the upright.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: and We bestowed upon him (offspring like) Isaac and Jacob, and bestowed prophethood and the Book on his descendants and granted him his reward in this world; he will certainly be among the righteous in the Hereafter.

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: And We bestowed on him, Ishaq and Ya`qub, and We ordained among his offspring prophethood and the Book, and We granted him his reward in this world; and verily, in the Hereafter he is indeed among the righteous.

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: And We bestowed on him Isaac and Jacob, and We established the prophethood and the Scripture among his seed, and We gave him his reward in the world, and lo! in the Hereafter he verily is among the righteous.

27.author: Qarai
