27.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and We ordained among his descendants prophethood and the Book, and We gave him his reward in this world, and in the Hereafter he will indeed be among the Righteous.

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: And, We gave him (Abraham), Isaac and Jacob and appointed on his descendants the Prophethood and the Book. We gave him his wage in this world, and in the Everlasting Life he shall be among the righteous.

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: And We gave to Him Isaac and Jacob and placed in his descendants prophethood and scripture. And We gave him his reward in this world, and indeed, he is in the Hereafter among the righteous.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: We granted Isaac and Jacob to Abraham and We bestowed upon his offspring, prophethood and the Book. We gave him his reward in this world and in the next life, he will be among the pious ones.

27.author: Shakir
27.text: And We granted him Ishaq and Yaqoub, and caused the prophethood and the book to remain in his seed, and We gave him his reward in this world, and in the hereafter he will most surely be among the good.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: We gave him Isaac and Jacob and granted prophethood and the Book to his descendants. We gave him his reward in this life and in the Hereafter he shall dwell among the righteous.

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: And We gave (Abraham) Isaac and Jacob, and ordained among his progeny Prophethood and Revelation, and We granted him his reward in this life; and he was in the Hereafter (of the company) of the Righteous.

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Wawahabna lahu ishaqa wayaAAqooba wajaAAalna fee thurriyyatihi alnnubuwwata waalkitaba waataynahu ajrahu fee alddunya wainnahu fee alakhirati lamina alssaliheena

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: And (remember) Lot when he said to his people: "You indulge in lecherous acts which none of the creatures had done before you.

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: And We rescued Lut when he said to his people, “You indeed commit the shameful; such that no one in the creation has ever done before you.”

28.author: Arberry
28.text: And Lot, when he said to his people 'Surely you commit such indecency as never any being in all the world committed before you.

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: And Lut: Recall what time he said unto his people: verily ye commit an indecency wherein none hath preceded you in the Worlds.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: And (remember) Lout (Lot), when he said to his people: "You commit Al-Fahishah (sodomy the worst sin) which none has preceded you in (committing) it in the 'Alamin (mankind and jinns)."

28.author: Itani
28.text: And Lot, when he said to his people, “You are committing an obscenity not perpetrated before you by anyone in the whole world.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: We sent Lot and he said to his people: “You commit the abomination that none in the world ever committed before you.

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: And (remember) Lut, when he said to his people: "You commit immoral sins which none has preceded you in (committing) it in all creatures."

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: And Lot! (Remember) when he said unto his folk: Lo! ye commit lewdness such as no creature did before you.

28.author: Qarai
28.text: And Lot, when he said to his people, ‘You indeed commit an indecency none in the world has ever committed before you!

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: And Lot who said to his nation: 'You commit indecency which no other being in all the world has committed before you.

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: And [mention] Lot, when he said to his people, "Indeed, you commit such immorality as no one has preceded you with from among the worlds.

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: Lot told his people, "You are certainly committing the kind of indecency which no one in the world has committed before".

28.author: Shakir
28.text: And (We sent) Lut when he said to his people: Most surely you are guilty of an indecency which none of the nations has ever done before you;

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: We sent Lot to his people and he said to them, "Indeed you commit obscenity such as no people before you have ever committed.

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: And (remember) Lut: behold, he said to his people: "Ye do commit lewdness, such as no people in Creation (ever) committed before you.

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: Walootan ith qala liqawmihi innakum latatoona alfahishata ma sabaqakum biha min ahadin mina alAAalameena

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: You commit unnatural acts with men and cut off the way (of procreation), and commit obscenities in your gatherings." The only answer his people made was: "Bring the punishment of God, if you are truthful."

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: “What! You commit the immoral with males, and cut off the roads; and you speak evilly in your gatherings?” So his people had no answer except to say, “Bring the punishment of Allah upon us if you are truthful!”

29.author: Arberry
29.text: What, do you approach men, and cut the way, and commit in your assembly dishonour?' But the only answer of his people was that they said, 'Then bring us the chastisement of God, if thou speakest truly.'

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: Ye go in indeed unto males, and ye rob on the highway, and ye commit that which is disreputable in your assembly? Then the answer of his people was naught but that they said: bring thou God's torment on us if thou art of the truth-tellers.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: "Verily, you do sodomy with men, and rob the wayfarer (travellers, etc.)! And practise Al-Munkar (disbelief and polytheism and every kind of evil wicked deed) in your meetings." But his people gave no answer except, that they said: "Bring Allah's Torment upon us if you are one of the truthful."

29.author: Itani
29.text: You approach men, and cut off the way, and commit lewdness in your gatherings.” But the only response from his people was to say, “Bring upon us God’s punishment, if you are truthful.”

29.author: Maududi
29.text: What! Do you go to men (to satisfy your lust), engage in highway robbery, and commit evil deeds in your gatherings?” Then they had no answer to offer other than to say: “Bring Allah's chastisement upon us if you are truthful.”

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: "Verily, you practice sodomy with men, and rob the wayfarer! And practice Al-Munkar in your meetings." But his people gave no answer except that they said: "Bring Allah's torment upon us if you are one of the truthful."

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: For come ye not in unto males, and cut ye not the road (for travellers), and commit ye not abomination in your meetings? But the answer of his folk was only that they said: Bring Allah's doom upon us if thou art a truthteller!

29.author: Qarai
29.text: What! Do you come to men, and cut off the way, and commit outrages in your gatherings?’ But the only answer of his people was that they said, ‘Bring down us Allah’s punishment, if you are truthful.’

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: What, do you approach men and cause a detour on the road, and commit dishonor (sodomy) in your assembly' But the only reply of his nation was: 'Then bring down the punishment of Allah, if what you say is true'

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: Indeed, you approach men and obstruct the road and commit in your meetings [every] evil." And the answer of his people was not but they said, "Bring us the punishment of Allah, if you should be of the truthful."

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: Do you engage in carnal relations with men, rob the travellers, and commit evil in your gatherings? His people had no answer but to say, "Bring upon us the torment of God if you are truthful".

29.author: Shakir
29.text: What! do you come to the males and commit robbery on the highway, and you commit evil deeds in your assemblies? But nothing was the answer of his people except that they said: Bring on us Allah's punishment, if you are one of the truthful.

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: You approach men and waylay them on the road and commit depravities within your gatherings." But his people's only reply was, "Bring down God's punishment upon us, if what you say be true."

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: "Do ye indeed approach men, and cut off the highway?- and practise wickedness (even) in your councils?" But his people gave no answer but this: they said: "Bring us the Wrath of Allah if thou tellest the truth."

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Ainnakum latatoona alrrijala wataqtaAAoona alssabeela watatoona fee nadeekumu almunkara fama kana jawaba qawmihi illa an qaloo itina biAAathabi Allahi in kunta mina alssadiqeena

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: "O Lord, help me against the wicked people,"(Lot) prayed.

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: He submitted, “My Lord! Help me against these mischievous people.”

30.author: Arberry
30.text: He said, 'My Lord, help me against the people that work corruption.'

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: He said: my Lord! give me victory over the corrupt people.

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: He said: "My Lord! Give me victory over the people who are Mufsidun (those who commit great crimes and sins, oppressors, tyrants, mischief-makers, corrupts).

30.author: Itani
30.text: He said, “My Lord, help me against the people of corruption.”

30.author: Maududi
30.text: Lot said: “My Lord, aid me against these mischievous people.”

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: He said: "My Lord! Give me victory over the people who are corrupt."

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: He said: My Lord! Give me victory over folk who work corruption.

30.author: Qarai
30.text: He said, ‘My Lord! Help me against this corruptive lot.’

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: 'My Lord' he said, 'help me against this corrupt nation'

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: He said, "My Lord, support me against the corrupting people."

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: He prayed, "Lord help me against the evil-doing people."

30.author: Shakir
30.text: He said: My Lord! help me against the mischievous people.

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: Lot prayed, "Lord, help me against this wicked people."

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: He said: "O my Lord! help Thou me against people who do mischief!"

30.author: Transliteration
30.text: Qala rabbi onsurnee AAala alqawmi almufsideena

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: When Our messengers came to Abraham with good news, they said: "We have to destroy this city as its inhabitants have become sinful."

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: And when Our sent angels came with glad tidings to Ibrahim, they said, “We will surely destroy the people of that town; indeed its inhabitants are unjust.”

31.author: Arberry
31.text: And when Our messengers came to Abraham with the good tidings, they said, 'We shall destroy the people of this city, for its people are evildoers.'

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: And when Our messengers came unto Ibrahim with the glad tidings, they said: verily we are about to destroy the inhabitants of that city: verily the inhabitants thereof have been wrong-doers.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: And when Our Messengers came to Ibrahim (Abraham) with the glad tidings they said: "Verily, we are going to destroy the people of this [Lout's (Lot's)] town (i.e. the town of Sodom in Palestine) truly, its people have been Zalimun [wrong-doers, polytheists and disobedient to Allah, and have also belied their Messenger Lout (Lot)]."

31.author: Itani
31.text: And when Our envoys brought Abraham the good news, they said, “We are going to destroy the people of this town; its people are wrongdoers.”

31.author: Maududi
31.text: When Our emissaries brought the good news to Abraham, and said (to him): “We are surely going to destroy the inhabitants of this city; its inhabitants are immersed in wrong-doing.”

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: And when Our messengers came to Ibrahim with the glad tidings they said: "Verily, we are going to destroy the people of this town; truly, its people have been wrongdoers."

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: And when Our messengers brought Abraham the good news, they said: Lo! we are about to destroy the people of that township, for its people are wrong-doers.

31.author: Qarai
31.text: And when Our messengers came to Abraham with the good news, they said, ‘We are indeed going to destroy the people of this town. Its people are indeed wrongdoers.’

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: And when Our Messengers brought Abraham the glad tidings they said: 'We are destroying the people of this village, because its people are harmdoers'

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: And when Our messengers came to Abraham with the good tidings, they said, "Indeed, we will destroy the people of that Lot's city. Indeed, its people have been wrongdoers."

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: When Our angelic Messengers brought glad news to Abraham, they told him, "We are about to destroy the people of this town for their injustice".

31.author: Shakir
31.text: And when Our apostles came to Ibrahim with the good news, they said: Surely we are going to destroy the people of this town, for its people are unjust.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: When Our messengers brought Abraham the good news [of the birth of Isaac] they [also] said, "We are indeed going to destroy the people of this city, for the people of this city are truly wrongdoers."

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: When Our Messengers came to Abraham with the good news, they said: "We are indeed going to destroy the people of this township: for truly they are (addicted to) crime."

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: Walamma jaat rusuluna ibraheema bialbushra qaloo inna muhlikoo ahli hathihi alqaryati inna ahlaha kanoo thalimeena

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: He said: "Surely Lot is there." They answered: "We know who is there. We are to save him and his family except his wife, for she is one of those who will stay behind."

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: He said, "Lut is in it!”; they said, “We know very well who all are there; we shall rescue him and his family, except his wife; she is of those who will stay behind.”

32.author: Arberry
32.text: He said, 'Lot is in it.' They said, 'We know very well who is in it; assuredly We shall deliver him and his family, except his wife; she has become of those that tarry.'

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: He said: verily Lut is therein. They said: we know better who is therein: we are to deliver him and his household, save his wife; she is to be of the lingerers.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: Ibrahim (Abraham) said: "But there is Lout (Lot) in it." They said:"We know better who is there, we will verily save him [Lout (Lot)] and his family, except his wife, she will be of those who remain behind (i.e. she will be destroyed along with those who will be destroyed from her folk)."

32.author: Itani
32.text: He said, “Yet Lot is in it.” They said, “We are well aware of who is in it. We will save him, and his family, except for his wife, who will remain behind.”

32.author: Maududi
32.text: Abraham said: “But Lot is there.” They replied: “We are well aware of those who are there. We shall save him and all his household except his wife.” His wife is among those who will stay behind.

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: Ibrahim said: "But there is Lut in it." They said: "We know better who is there. We will verily, save him and his family - except his wife, she will be of those who remain behind."

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: He said: Lo! Lot is there. They said: We are best aware of who is there. We are to deliver him and his household, all save his wife, who is of those who stay behind.

32.author: Qarai
32.text: He said, ‘Lot is in it.’ They said, ‘We know better those who are in it. We will surely deliver him and his family, except his wife: she shall be one of those who remain behind.’

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: He said: 'Lot is in it' They replied: 'We know who are in it, we shall save him and his family, except his wife, she has become of those that shall remain behind'

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: [Abraham] said, "Indeed, within it is Lot." They said, "We are more knowing of who is within it. We will surely save him and his family, except his wife. She is to be of those who remain behind."

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: Abraham said, "Lot is there in that town!" They said, "We know everyone there. We shall certainly save him and his family except his wife who will remain behind."

32.author: Shakir
32.text: He said: Surely in it is Lut. They said: We know well who is in it; we shall certainly deliver him and his followers, except his wife; she shall be of those who remain behind.

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: Abraham said, "But, Lot lives here." They answered, "We well know who lives here. We shall surely save him and his whole family, except his wife, who will be among those who stay behind."

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: He said: "But there is Lut there." They said: "Well do we know who is there: we will certainly save him and his following,- except his wife: she is of those who lag behind!"

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Qala inna feeha lootan qaloo nahnu aAAlamu biman feeha lanunajjiyannahu waahlahu illa imraatahu kanat mina alghabireena

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: So, when Our messengers came to Lot, he was worried on their account as he was unable to protect them. They said: "Have no fear or regret. We will certainly save you and your family except your wife, for she is one of those who will stay behind.

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: And when Our sent angels came to Lut, he was unhappy at their arrival and was depressed – and they said, “Do not fear or grieve; we will surely rescue you and your family, except your wife, who is of those who will stay behind.”

33.author: Arberry
33.text: When that Our messengers came to Lot he was troubled on their account and distressed for them; but they said, 'Fear not, neither sorrow, for surely we shall deliver thee and thy family, except thy wife; she has become of those that tarry.

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: And when Our messengers came unto Lut, he was distressed on their account and felt straitened on their account. And they said: fear not, nor grieve: verily we are to deliver thee and thy household, save thy wife: she is to be of the lingerers.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: And when Our Messengers came to Lout (Lot), he was grieved because of them, and felt straitened on their account. They said: "Have no fear, and do not grieve! Truly, we shall save you and your family, except your wife, she will be of those who remain behind (i.e. she will be destroyed along with those who will be destroyed from her folk).

33.author: Itani
33.text: Then, when Our envoys came to Lot, they were mistreated, and he was troubled and distressed on their account. They said, “Do not fear, nor grieve. We will save you and your family, except for your wife, who will remain behind.”

33.author: Maududi
33.text: When Our emissaries came to Lot he was distressed and embarrassed on their account. They said: “Do not fear nor be distressed. We shall save you and all your household except your wife who is among those that will stay behind.

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: And when Our messengers came to Lut, he was grieved because of them, and felt straitened on their account. They said: "Have no fear, and do not grieve! Truly, we shall save you and your family except your wife: she will be of those who remain behind."

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: And when Our messengers came unto Lot, he was troubled upon their account, for he could not protect them; but they said: Fear not, nor grieve! Lo! we are to deliver thee and thy household, (all) save thy wife, who is of those who stay behind.

33.author: Qarai
33.text: And when Our messengers came to Lot, he was distressed on their account and in a predicament for their sake. But they said, ‘Do not be afraid, nor grieve! We shall deliver you and your family, except your wife: she will be one of those who remain behind.

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: And when Our Messengers came to Lot, he was troubled and distressed on their account. But they said: 'Have no fear, and do not be sad. Surely, we will save you and your family except your wife, she has become of those that shall remain behind'

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: And when Our messengers came to Lot, he was distressed for them and felt for them great discomfort. They said, "Fear not, nor grieve. Indeed, we will save you and your family, except your wife; she is to be of those who remain behind.

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: When Our angelic Messengers came to Lot, he was grieved and depressed to see them. They told him, "Do not be afraid or grieved. We will rescue you and your family except your wife who will remain behind.

33.author: Shakir
33.text: And when Our apostles came to Lut he was grieved on account of them, and he felt powerless (to protect) them; and they said: Fear not, nor grieve; surely we will deliver you and your followers, except your wife; she shall be of those who remain behind.

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: When Our messengers came to Lot, he was troubled and distressed on their account. They said, "Have no fear or grief. We shall certainly save you and your household, except your wife, who will be among those who stay behind,

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: And when Our Messengers came to Lut, he was grieved on their account, and felt himself powerless (to protect) them: but they said: "Fear thou not, nor grieve: we are (here) to save thee and thy following, except thy wife: she is of those who lag behind.

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: Walamma an jaat rusuluna lootan seea bihim wadaqa bihim tharAAan waqaloo la takhaf wala tahzan inna munajjooka waahlaka illa imraataka kanat mina alghabireena

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: We have to bring a scourge from the heavens on the people of this city as they are depraved."

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: “We will indeed cause a punishment from the sky to descend upon the inhabitants of this town – the recompense of their disobedience.”

34.author: Arberry
34.text: We shall send down upon the people of this city wrath out of heaven for their ungodliness.'

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: Verily we are about to bring down upon the inhabitants of this city a scourge from the heaven, for they have been transgressing.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: Verily, we are about to bring down on the people of this town a great torment from the sky, because they have been rebellious (against Allah's Command)."

34.author: Itani
34.text: “We will bring down upon the people of this town a scourge from heaven, because of their wickedness.”

34.author: Maududi
34.text: We shall bring down upon the people of this city a scourge from the heaven because of their evildoing.”

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: "Verily, we are about to bring down on the people of this town a great torment from the sky, because they have been rebellious."

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: Lo! We are about to bring down upon the folk of this township a fury from the sky because they are evil-livers.

34.author: Qarai
34.text: We are indeed going to bring down upon the people of this town a punishment from the sky because of the transgressions they used to commit.’

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: We shall send down anger out of heaven upon the people of this city because they are debauchers.

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: Indeed, we will bring down on the people of this city punishment from the sky because they have been defiantly disobedient."

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: We will bring torment from the sky on this town because of the evil-deeds of its inhabitants".

34.author: Shakir
34.text: Surely We will cause to come down upon the people of this town a punishment from heaven, because they transgressed.

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: We are surely going to bring down a punishment from heaven on the people of this town because of their depravities."

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: "For we are going to bring down on the people of this township a Punishment from heaven, because they have been wickedly rebellious."

34.author: Transliteration
34.text: Inna munziloona AAala ahli hathihi alqaryati rijzan mina alssamai bima kanoo yafsuqoona

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: Verily We have left a clear sign of this for people of sense to see.

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: And We have undoubtedly kept a part of it as a clear sign for people of intellect.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: And indeed, We have left thereof a sign, a clear sign? unto a people who understand.

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: And assuredly We have left thereof manifest sign for a people who reflect.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: And indeed We have left thereof an evident Ayah (a lesson and a warning and a sign the place where the Dead Sea is now in Palestine) for a folk who understand.

35.author: Itani
35.text: And We left behind a clear trace of it, for people who understand.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: And We have left a vestige of it in that city as a Clear Sign for a people who use their reason.

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: And indeed We have left thereof an evident Ayah for a folk who understand.

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: And verily of that We have left a clear sign for people who have sense.

35.author: Qarai
35.text: Certainly We have left of it a manifest sign for people who exercise their reason.

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: Indeed, We left a clear sign for a nation that understands.

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: And We have certainly left of it a sign as clear evidence for a people who use reason.

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: We left manifest evidence (of the truth) there for the people of understanding.

35.author: Shakir
35.text: And certainly We have left a clear sign of it for a people who understand.

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: Surely the ruins We left of that city are a clear sign for a people who use their reason.

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: And We have left thereof an evident Sign, for any people who (care to) understand.

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: Walaqad tarakna minha ayatan bayyinatan liqawmin yaAAqiloona

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: To Midian We sent their brother Shu'aib. He said: "O people, worship God and be ready for the Day of Resurrection. Do no evil, and create no mischief in the land."

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: And We sent towards Madyan, their fellowman Shuaib – he therefore said, “O my people! Worship Allah, and anticipate the Last Day, and do not roam the earth spreading turmoil.”

36.author: Arberry
36.text: And to Midian their brother Shuaib; he said, 'O my people, serve God, and look you for the Last Day; and do not mischief in the land, working corruption.'

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: And unto Madyan We sent their brother, Shu'aib. He said: my people! worship Allah, and fear the Last Day, and commit not evil on the earth as corrupters.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: And to (the people of) Madyan (Midian), We sent their brother Shu'aib (Shuaib). He said: "O my people! Worship Allah, and hope for (the reward of good deeds by worshipping Allah Alone, on) the last Day, and commit no mischief on the earth as Mufsidun (those who commit great crimes, oppressors, tyrants, mischief-makers, corrupts).

36.author: Itani
36.text: And to Median, their brother Shuaib. He said, “O my people, worship God and anticipate the Last Day, and do not spread corruption in the land.”

36.author: Maududi
36.text: And We sent to Midian their brother Shuayb. He said: “My people, serve Allah and look forward to the Last Day and do not go about the earth committing mischief.”

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: And to Madyan, We sent their brother Shu`ayb. He said: "O my people! Worship Allah and hope for the last Day, and commit no mischief on the earth as mischief-makers."

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: And unto Midian We sent Shu'eyb, their brother. He said: O my people! Serve Allah, and look forward to the Last Day, and do not evil, making mischief, in the earth.

36.author: Qarai
36.text: And to Midian We sent Shu‘ayb, their brother. He said, ‘O my people! Worship Allah, and expect [to encounter] the Last Day, and do not act wickedly on the earth causing corruption.’

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: And to Midian their brother Shu'aib. He said: 'Worship Allah, my nation. Look to the Last Day. Do not make mischief in the land, doing corrupt works.

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: And to Madyan [We sent] their brother Shu'ayb, and he said, "O my people, worship Allah and expect the Last Day and do not commit abuse on the earth, spreading corruption."

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: We sent to the people of Midian their brother Shu`ayb. He told them, "Worship God. Have hope in the life to come. Do not spread evil in the land".

36.author: Shakir
36.text: And to Madyan (We sent) their brother Shuaib, so he said: O my people! serve Allah and fear the latter day and do not act corruptly in the land, making mischief.

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: To the people of Midian We sent their brother Shu'ayb. He said, "My people, worship God, and look forward to the Last Day, and do not commit evil and spread corruption in the land."

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: To the Madyan (people) (We sent) their brother Shu'aib. Then he said: "O my people! serve Allah, and fear the Last Day: nor commit evil on the earth, with intent to do mischief."

36.author: Transliteration
36.text: Waila madyana akhahum shuAAayban faqala ya qawmi oAAbudoo Allaha waorjoo alyawma alakhira wala taAAthaw fee alardi mufsideena

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: But they denied him and were seized by an earthquake, and lay overturned in their homes in the morning.

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: In response they denied him – thereupon the earthquake seized them – so at morning they remained lying flattened in their homes.

37.author: Arberry
37.text: But they cried lies to him; so the earthquake seized them, and morning found them in their habitation fallen prostrate.

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: Then they belied him; wherefore an earthquake laid hold of them, and they lay in their dwellings, crouching.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: And they belied him [Shu'aib (Shuaib)], so the earthquake seized them, and they lay (dead), prostrate in their dwellings.

37.author: Itani
37.text: But they rejected him, so the tremor overtook them, and they were left motionless in their homes.

37.author: Maududi
37.text: But they denounced him as a liar. So a mighty earthquake overtook them, and by the morning they lay overturned in their houses.

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: And they denied him; so the earthquake seized them, and they lay, prostrate in their dwellings.

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: But they denied him, and the dreadful earthquake took them, and morning found them prostrate in their dwelling place.

37.author: Qarai
37.text: But they impugned him, whereupon the earthquake seized them, and they lay lifeless prostrate in their homes.

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: But they belied him, so the earthquake seized them, and when morning came, they were found fallen crouched in their dwellings, dead.

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: But they denied him, so the earthquake seized them, and they became within their home [corpses] fallen prone.

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: They rejected him so We jolted them with a violent earthquake and they were left motionless in their houses.

37.author: Shakir
37.text: But they rejected him, so a severe earthquake overtook them, and they became motionless bodies in their abode.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: But they rejected him, so the earthquake overwhelmed them and they were left lying prostrate on the ground in their homes.

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: But they rejected him: Then the mighty Blast seized them, and they lay prostrate in their homes by the morning.

37.author: Transliteration
37.text: Fakaththaboohu faakhathathumu alrrajfatu faasbahoo fee darihim jathimeena

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: And (remember) 'Ad and Thamud. It will be clear to you from their habitations (how they were destroyed), for Satan had made their deeds look attractive to them, and turned them away from the path; and yet they were a people of acumen.

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: And We destroyed the A’ad and the Thamud, and you already know their dwellings; Satan made their deeds appear good to them and prevented them from the path, whereas they could perceive.

38.author: Arberry
38.text: And Ad, and Thamood -- it has become clear to you from their dwelling-places; and Satan decked out fair to them their works, and barred them from the way, though they saw clearly.

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: And the 'Aad and Thamud and of a surety their destruction is apparent unto you from their dwellings. The Satan made fairseeming their works unto them, and so kept them off from the path, while they were endued with sight.

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: And 'Ad and Thamud (people)! And indeed (their destruction) is clearly apparent to you from their (ruined) dwellings. Shaitan (Satan) made their deeds fair-seeming to them, and turned them away from the (Right) Path, though they were intelligent.

38.author: Itani
38.text: And Aad and Thamood. It has become clear to you from their dwellings. Satan embellished for them their deeds, barring them from the path, even though they could see.

38.author: Maududi
38.text: And We destroyed Ad and Thamud, whose dwellings you have observed. Satan had embellished their deeds for them and had turned them away from the Right Path although they were a people of clear perception.

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: And `Ad and Thamud! And indeed (their destruction) is clearly apparent to you from their (ruined) dwellings. Shaytan made their deeds fair seeming to them, and turned them away from the path, though they were intelligent.

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: And (the tribes of) A'ad and Thamud! (Their fate) is manifest unto you from their (ruined and deserted) dwellings. Satan made their deeds seem fair unto them and so debarred them from the Way, though they were keen observers.

38.author: Qarai
38.text: And ‘Ad and Thamud, [whose fate] is evident to you from their habitations. Satan made their deeds seem decorous to them, thus he barred them from the way [of Allah], though they used to be perceptive.

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: Aad and Thamood, it has become clear to you from their dwellings; satan made their works seem fair to them and barred them from the Path, although they saw clearly.

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: And [We destroyed] 'Aad and Thamud, and it has become clear to you from their [ruined] dwellings. And Satan had made pleasing to them their deeds and averted them from the path, and they were endowed with perception.

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: How the people of Ad and Thamud were destroyed is evident to you from their homes. Satan made their deeds seem attractive to them and prevented them from the right path, even though they had visions.

38.author: Shakir
38.text: And (We destroyed) Ad and Samood, and from their dwellings (this) is apparent to you indeed; and the Shaitan made their deeds fair-seeming to them, so he kept them back from the path, though they were endowed with intelligence and skill,

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: The same happened to the tribes of 'Ad and Thamud: this must be clear to you from their ruins. Satan made their actions seem good to them, and turned them away from the straight path, even though they were intelligent people.

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: (Remember also) the 'Ad and the Thamud (people): clearly will appear to you from (the traces) of their buildings (their fate): the Evil One made their deeds alluring to them, and kept them back from the Path, though they were gifted with intelligence and skill.

38.author: Transliteration
38.text: WaAAadan wathamooda waqad tabayyana lakum min masakinihim wazayyana lahumu alshshaytanu aAAmalahum fasaddahum AAani alssabeeli wakanoo mustabsireena

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: (Remember) Qarun, Pharaoh and Haman to whom Moses came with clear signs; but they were haughty (and oppressed) the land. Yet they could not run away from Us;

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: And (We destroyed) Qaroon, Firaun and Haman; and indeed Moosa came to them with clear signs, so they were arrogant in the land, and they could never escape from Our control.

39.author: Arberry
39.text: And Korah, and Pharaoh, and Haman; Moses came to them with the clear signs, but they waxed proud in the earth, yet they outstripped Us not.

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: And Qarun and Fir'awn and Haman! And assuredly Musa came unto them with the evidences, yet they were stiff-necked in the land. And they could not outstrip Us.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: And (We destroyed also) Qarun (Korah), Fir'aun (Pharaoh), and Haman. And indeed Musa (Moses) came to them with clear Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), but they were arrogant in the land, yet they could not outstrip Us (escape Our punishment).

39.author: Itani
39.text: And Quaroon, and Pharaoh, and Hamaan—Moses went to them with clear arguments, but they acted arrogantly in the land. And they could not get ahead.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: And We destroyed Qarun (Korah) and Pharaoh and Haman. Moses came to them with Clear Signs but they waxed arrogant in the land although they could not have outstripped (Us).

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: And Qarun, Fir`awn, and Haman. And indeed Musa came to them with clear Ayat, but they were arrogant in the land, yet they could not outstrip Us.

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: And Korah, Pharaoh and Haman! Moses came unto them with clear proofs (of Allah's Sovereignty), but they were boastful in the land. And they were not winners (in the race).

39.author: Qarai
39.text: And Korah, Pharaoh, and Haman. Certainly, Moses brought them manifest proofs, but they acted arrogantly in the land; though they could not outmaneuver [Allah].

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: And Korah, Pharaoh, and Haman Moses came to them with clear signs, but they became very insolent in the earth, yet they did not outstrip Us.

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: And [We destroyed] Qarun and Pharaoh and Haman. And Moses had already come to them with clear evidences, and they were arrogant in the land, but they were not outrunners [of Our punishment].

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: Korah, the Pharaoh, and Haman were also destroyed. Moses had brought them illustrious miracles, but they were puffed-up with pride in the land and they could not defeat Us.

39.author: Shakir
39.text: And (We destroyed) Qaroun and Firon and Haman; and certainly Musa came to them with clear arguments, but they behaved haughtily in the land; yet they could not outstrip (Us).

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: Korah, Pharaoh, and Haman: Moses came to them with clear signs but they were arrogant in the land. They could not escape Us.

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: (Remember also) Qarun, Pharaoh, and Haman: there came to them Moses with Clear Signs, but they behaved with insolence on the earth; yet they could not overreach (Us).

39.author: Transliteration
39.text: Waqaroona wafirAAawna wahamana walaqad jaahum moosa bialbayyinati faistakbaroo fee alardi wama kanoo sabiqeena

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: And We seized all of them for their crimes. Against some We sent a violent wind hurling stones, and some We seized with a mighty blast, and some We submerged under the earth, and some We drowned. It was not for God to wrong them, they wronged themselves.

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: So We caught each one upon his sin; so among them is one upon whom We sent a shower of stones; and among them is one who was seized by the Scream; and among them is one whom We buried in the earth, and among them is one whom We drowned; it did not befit Allah’s Majesty to oppress them, but they were wronging themselves.

40.author: Arberry
40.text: Each We seized for his sin; and of them against some We loosed a squall of pebbles and some were seized by the Cry, and some We made the earth to swallow, and some We drowned; God would never wrong them, but they wronged themselves.

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: Wherefore each We laid hold of for his sin. Of them were some on whom We sent a violent wind; and of them were some - who were overtaken by a shout; and of them were some - with whom We sank the earth; and of them were some - whom We drowned. Allah was not such as to wrong them, but themselves they were wont to wrong.

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: So We punished each (of them) for his sins, of them were some on whom We sent Hasiban (a violent wind with shower of stones) [as the people of Lout (Lot)], and of them were some who were overtaken by As-Saihah [torment - awful cry, etc. (as Thamud or Shu'aib's people)], and of them were some whom We caused the earth to swallow [as Qarun (Korah)], and of them were some whom We drowned [as the people of Nuh (Noah), or Fir'aun (Pharaoh) and his people]. It was not Allah Who wronged them, but they wronged themselves.

40.author: Itani
40.text: Each We seized by his sin. Against some We sent a sandstorm. Some were struck by the Blast. Some We caused the ground to cave in beneath them. And some We drowned. It was not God who wronged them, but it was they who wronged their own selves.

40.author: Maududi
40.text: So We seized each for their sin. We let loose upon some a violent tornado with showers of stones; some were overtaken by a mighty Cry; some were caused to be swallowed up by the earth, and some We drowned. Allah would not wrong them, but it is they who wronged themselves.

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: So, We punished each for his sins, of them were some on whom We sent a Hasib, and of them were some who were overtaken by As-Sayhah, and of them were some whom We caused the earth to swallow, and of them were some whom We drowned. It was not Allah Who wronged them, but they wronged themselves.

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: So We took each one in his sin; of them was he on whom We sent a hurricane, and of them was he who was overtaken by the (Awful) Cry, and of them was he whom We caused the earth to swallow, and of them was he whom We drowned. It was not for Allah to wrong them, but they wronged themselves.

40.author: Qarai
40.text: So We seized each [of them] for his sin: among them were those upon whom We unleashed a rain of stones, and among them were those who were seized by the Cry, and among them were those whom We caused the earth to swallow, and among them were those whom We drowned. It was not Allah who wronged them, but it was they who used to wrong themselves.

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: Each of them We seized for his sin. On some We loosed a squall of pebbles, and others were seized by the Cry. Some We caused to be swallowed up by the earth, and some We drowned. Allah would never wrong them but they wronged themselves.

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: So each We seized for his sin; and among them were those upon whom We sent a storm of stones, and among them were those who were seized by the blast [from the sky], and among them were those whom We caused the earth to swallow, and among them were those whom We drowned. And Allah would not have wronged them, but it was they who were wronging themselves.

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: We punished all of these people because of their sin. Some of them were struck by a violent sand-storm, some by a blast of sound, others were swallowed up by the earth, and some were drowned (in the sea). God did not do injustice to them, but they had wronged themselves.

40.author: Shakir
40.text: So each We punished for his sin; of them was he on whom We sent down a violent storm, and of them was he whom the rumbling overtook, and of them was he whom We made to be swallowed up by the earth, and of them was he whom We drowned; and it did not beseem Allah that He should be unjust to them, but they were unjust to their own souls.

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: So We seized each one for his sins; some We struck with a violent storm; some of them were overcome by a sudden blast, some were swallowed up by the earth and some We drowned. God did not wrong them: they wronged themselves.

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: Each one of them We seized for his crime: of them, against some We sent a violent tornado (with showers of stones); some were caught by a (mighty) Blast; some We caused the earth to swallow up; and some We drowned (in the waters): It was not Allah Who injured (or oppressed) them:" They injured (and oppressed) their own souls.

40.author: Transliteration
40.text: Fakullan akhathna bithanbihi faminhum man arsalna AAalayhi hasiban waminhum man akhathathu alssayhatu waminhum man khasafna bihi alarda waminhum man aghraqna wama kana Allahu liyathlimahum walakin kanoo anfusahum yathlimoona

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: The semblance of those who take protectors besides God is that of the spider. She arranges a house for herself, but the flimsiest of houses is the spider's. If only they had sense!

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: The example of those who choose masters other than Allah is like that of the spider; it makes the web its house; and indeed the weakest house of all is that of the spider; if only they knew.

41.author: Arberry
41.text: The likeness of those who have taken to them protectors, apart from God, is as the likeness of the spider that takes to itself a house; and surely the frailest of houses is the house of the spider, did they but know.

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: The likeness of those who take other patrons than Allah is as the likeness of the spider who taketh unto herself a house. And verily the frailest of all houses is the spider's house - if they but knew!

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: The likeness of those who take Auliya' (protectors and helpers) other than Allah is as the likeness of a spider, who builds (for itself) a house, but verily, the frailest (weakest) of houses is the spider's house; if they but knew.

41.author: Itani
41.text: The likeness of those who take to themselves protectors other than God is that of the spider. It builds a house. But the most fragile of houses is the spider’s house. If they only knew.

41.author: Maududi
41.text: The case of those who took others than Allah as their protectors is that of a spider who builds a house; but the frailest of all houses is the spider's house; if they only knew.

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: The parable of those who seek protectors from other than Allah is that of a spider who builds a house; but indeed, the weakest of houses is the spider's house -- if they but knew.

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: The likeness of those who choose other patrons than Allah is as the likeness of the spider when she taketh unto herself a house, and lo! the frailest of all houses is the spider's house, if they but knew.

41.author: Qarai
41.text: The parable of those who take guardians instead of Allah is that of the spider that takes a home, and indeed the frailest of homes is the home of a spider, had they known!

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: The likeness of those who have taken guardians, other than Allah, is as the likeness of the spider that takes to itself a house; surely the spider's house is the weakest house if they but knew.

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: The example of those who take allies other than Allah is like that of the spider who takes a home. And indeed, the weakest of homes is the home of the spider, if they only knew.

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: The belief of considering other things as one's guardians besides God is as feeble as a spider's web. The spider's web is the frailest of all dwellings, if only they knew it.

41.author: Shakir
41.text: The parable of those who take guardians besides Allah is as the parable of the spider that makes for itself a house; and most surely the frailest of the houses is the spider's house did they but know.

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: Those who take protectors other than God can be compared to the spider which builds itself a cobweb, but the frailest of all structures is the house of the spider, if they but knew it.

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: The parable of those who take protectors other than Allah is that of the spider, who builds (to itself) a house; but truly the flimsiest of houses is the spider's house;- if they but knew.

41.author: Transliteration
41.text: Mathalu allatheena ittakhathoo min dooni Allahi awliyaa kamathali alAAankabooti ittakhathat baytan wainna awhana albuyooti labaytu alAAankabooti law kanoo yaAAlamoona

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: Verily God knows what they invoke in His place, for He is all-mighty and all-wise.

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: Allah knows what they worship instead of Him; He is the Almighty, the Wise.

42.author: Arberry
42.text: God knows whatever thing they call upon apart from Him; He is the All-mighty, the All-wise.

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: Verily Allah knoweth whatsoever thing they invoke beside Him. And He is the Mighty, the Wise.

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: Verily, Allah knows what things they invoke instead of Him. He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

42.author: Itani
42.text: God knows what they invoke besides Him. He is the Almighty, the Wise.

42.author: Maududi
42.text: Surely Allah knows fully what they call upon apart from Him. He is the Most Powerful, the Most Wise.

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: Verily, Allah knows what things they invoke instead of Him. He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: Lo! Allah knoweth what thing they invoke instead of Him. He is the Mighty, the Wise.

42.author: Qarai
42.text: Allah indeed knows whatever thing they invoke besides Him, and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise.

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: Allah knows whatever they call upon other than Him; He is the Almighty, the Wise.

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: Indeed, Allah knows whatever thing they call upon other than Him. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: God knows whatever they worship besides Him; He is the Majestic and All-wise.

42.author: Shakir
42.text: Surely Allah knows whatever thing they call upon besides Him; and He is the Mighty, the Wise.

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: God surely knows what they invoke besides Him. He is the Mighty, the Wise One.

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: Verily Allah doth know of (every thing) whatever that they call upon besides Him: and He is Exalted (in power), Wise.

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: Inna Allaha yaAAlamu ma yadAAoona min doonihi min shayin wahuwa alAAazeezu alhakeemu

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: These are precepts of wisdom We offer to men, but only those who are rational understand.

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: And We illustrate these examples for mankind; and none except the knowledgeable understand them.

43.author: Arberry
43.text: And those similitudes -- We strike them for the people, but none understands them save those who know.

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: And these similtudes! We propound them for mankind; and none understand them save men of knowledge.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: And these similitudes We put forward for mankind, but none will understand them except those who have knowledge (of Allah and His Signs, etc.).

43.author: Itani
43.text: These examples—We put them forward to the people; but none grasps them except the learned.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: These are the parables that We set forth to make people understand. But only those endowed with knowledge will comprehend them.

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: And these are the examples We give for mankind; but none will understand them except those who have knowledge (of Allah).

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: As for these similitudes, We coin them for mankind, but none will grasp their meaning save the wise.

43.author: Qarai
43.text: We draw these parables for mankind; but no one grasps them except those who have knowledge.

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: And We strike these parables for the people, but none understands except the knowledgeable.

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: And these examples We present to the people, but none will understand them except those of knowledge.

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: These are parables which We tell to human being, but only the learned ones understand them.

43.author: Shakir
43.text: And (as for) these examples, We set them forth for men, and none understand them but the learned.

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: Such are the comparisons We make for people, but only those understand them who have knowledge.

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: And such are the Parables We set forth for mankind, but only those understand them who have knowledge.

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: Watilka alamthalu nadribuha lilnnasi wama yaAAqiluha illa alAAalimoona

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: God has created the heavens and the earth with reason. Surely in this is a sign for those who believe.

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: Allah created the heavens and the earth with the truth; indeed in this is a sign for the Muslims.

44.author: Arberry
44.text: God created the heavens and the earth with the truth; surely in that is a sign to the believers.

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: Allah hath created the heavens and the earth in truth; verily there in is a sign for the believers.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: (Allah says to His Prophet Muhammad SAW): "Allah (Alone) created the heavens and the earth with truth (and none shared Him in their creation)." Verily! Therein is surely a sign for those who believe.

44.author: Itani
44.text: God created the heavens and the earth with truth. Surely in that is a sign for the believers.

44.author: Maududi
44.text: Allah has created the heavens and the earth in Truth. Certainly there is a Sign in this for those who believe.

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: Allah created the heavens and the earth with truth. Verily, therein is surely a sign for those who believe.

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: Allah created the heavens and the earth with truth. Lo! therein is indeed a portent for believers.

44.author: Qarai
44.text: Allah created the heavens and the earth with consummate wisdom. There is indeed a sign in that for the faithful.

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: Allah has created the heavens and the earth with the truth. Surely, in that there is a sign for believers.

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: Allah created the heavens and the earth in truth. Indeed in that is a sign for the believers.

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: God has created the heavens and the earth for a genuine purpose. In this there is evidence (of the truth) for the believers.

44.author: Shakir
44.text: Allah created the heavens and the earth with truth; most surely there is a sign in this for the believers.

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: God has created the heavens and the earth for a purpose; surely in this there is a sign for true believers.

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: Allah created the heavens and the earth in true (proportions): verily in that is a Sign for those who believe.

44.author: Transliteration
44.text: Khalaqa Allahu alssamawati waalarda bialhaqqi inna fee thalika laayatan lilmumineena

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: Recite what has been revealed to you of this Book, and be constant in devotion. Surely prayer keeps you away from the obscene and detestable, but the remembrance of God is greater far; and God knows what you do.

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: O dear Prophet (Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), recite from the Book which has been sent down to you, and establish the prayer; indeed the prayer stops from indecency and evil; and indeed the remembrance of Allah is the greatest; and Allah knows all what you do.

45.author: Arberry
45.text: Recite what has been revealed to thee of the Book, and perform the prayer; prayer forbids indecency and dishonour. God's remembrance is greater; and God knows the things you work.

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: Recite thou that which hath been revealed unto thee of the Book and establish thou the prayer; verily prayer preventeth man from indecency and that which is disreputable: and surely the remembrance of Allah is the greatest. And Allah knoweth that which ye perform.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: Recite (O Muhammad SAW) what has been revealed to you of the Book (the Quran), and perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat). Verily, As-Salat (the prayer) prevents from Al-Fahsha' (i.e. great sins of every kind, unlawful sexual intercourse, etc.) and Al-Munkar (i.e. disbelief, polytheism, and every kind of evil wicked deed, etc.) and the remembering (praising, etc.) of (you by) Allah (in front of the angels) is greater indeed [than your remembering (praising, etc.) Allah in prayers, etc.]. And Allah knows what you do.

45.author: Itani
45.text: Recite what is revealed to you of the Scripture, and perform the prayer. The prayer prevents indecencies and evils. And the remembrance of God is greater. And God knows what you do.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: (O Prophet), recite the Book that has been revealed to you and establish Prayer. Surely Prayer forbids indecency and evil. And Allah's remembrance is of even greater merit. Allah knows all that you do.

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: Recite what has been revealed to you of the Book, and perform the Salah. Verily, the Salah prevents from Al-Fahsha' (immoral sins) and Al-Munkar (evil deeds) and the remembering (praising) of Allah is greater indeed. And Allah knows what you do.

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: Recite that which hath been inspired in thee of the Scripture, and establish worship. Lo! worship preserveth from lewdness and iniquity, but verily remembrance of Allah is more important. And Allah knoweth what ye do.

45.author: Qarai
45.text: Recite what has been revealed to you of the Book, and maintain the prayer. Indeed the prayer restrains from indecent and wrongful conduct, and the remembrance of Allah is surely greater. And Allah knows whatever [deeds] you do.

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: Recite what has been sent down to you of the Book, and establish the prayer. Prayer forbids indecency and dishonor. The remembrance of Allah is greater, and Allah knows what you do.

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: Recite, [O Muhammad], what has been revealed to you of the Book and establish prayer. Indeed, prayer prohibits immorality and wrongdoing, and the remembrance of Allah is greater. And Allah knows that which you do.

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: (Muhammad), recite to them what has been revealed to you in the Book and be steadfast in prayer; prayer keeps one away from indecency and evil. It is the greatest act of worshipping God. God knows what you do.

45.author: Shakir
45.text: Recite that which has been revealed to you of the Book and keep up prayer; surely prayer keeps (one) away from indecency and evil, and certainly the remembrance of Allah is the greatest, and Allah knows what you do.

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: Recite what has been revealed to you of the book, and pray regularly. Surely prayer restrains one from indecency and evil and remembrance of God is greater. God has knowledge of all your actions.

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: Recite what is sent of the Book by inspiration to thee, and establish regular Prayer: for Prayer restrains from shameful and unjust deeds; and remembrance of Allah is the greatest (thing in life) without doubt. And Allah knows the (deeds) that ye do.

45.author: Transliteration
45.text: Otlu ma oohiya ilayka mina alkitabi waaqimi alssalata inna alssalata tanha AAani alfahshai waalmunkari walathikru Allahi akbaru waAllahu yaAAlamu ma tasnaAAoona

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: Do not argue with the people of the Book unless in a fair way, apart from those who act wrongly, and say to them: "We believe what has been sent down to us, and we believe what has been sent down to you. Our God and your God is one, and to Him we submit."

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: And O Muslims, do not argue with the People given the Book(s) except in the best manner – except (with) those among them who oppress, and say, “We believe in what has been sent down towards us and what has been sent towards you – and ours and your God is One, and we have submitted ourselves to Him.”

46.author: Arberry
46.text: Dispute not with the People of the Book save in the fairer manner, except for those of them that do wrong; and say, 'We believe in what has been sent down to us, and what has been sent down to you; our God and your God is One, and to Him we have surrendered.'

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: And dispute not with the people of the Book unless in the best manner, save with those of them who do wrong; and say: we believe in that which hath been sent down unto us and that which hath been sent down unto you: our God and your God is One; and unto Him we are submissive.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: And argue not with the people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians), unless it be in (a way) that is better (with good words and in good manner, inviting them to Islamic Monotheism with His Verses), except with such of them as do wrong, and say (to them): "We believe in that which has been revealed to us and revealed to you; our Ilah (God) and your Ilah (God) is One (i.e. Allah), and to Him we have submitted (as Muslims)."

46.author: Itani
46.text: And do not argue with the People of the Scripture except in the best manner possible, except those who do wrong among them. And say, “We believe in what was revealed to us, and in what was revealed to you; and our God and your God is One; and to Him we are submissive.”

46.author: Maududi
46.text: Argue not with the People of the Book except in the fairest manner, unless it be those of them that are utterly unjust. Say to them: “We believe in what was revealed to us and what was revealed to you. One is our God and your God; and we are those who submit ourselves to Him.”

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: And argue not with the People of the Scripture, except with that which is better -- except with such of them as do wrong; and say (to them): "We believe in that which has been revealed to us and revealed to you; our God and your God is One, and to Him we have submitted."

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: And argue not with the People of the Scripture unless it be in (a way) that is better, save with such of them as do wrong; and say: We believe in that which hath been revealed unto us and revealed unto you; our Allah and your Allah is One, and unto Him we surrender.

46.author: Qarai
46.text: Do not argue with the People of the Book except in a manner which is best, except such of them as are wrongdoers, and say, ‘We believe in what has been sent down to us and in what has been sent down to you; our God and your God is one [and the same], and to Him do we submit.’

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: And do not dispute with the People of the Book (Nazarenes) except in the best manner, except for those among them who do wrong and say (to them): 'We believe in that which was sent down to us and that which was sent down to you. Our God and your God is One, and to Him we have surrendered'

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: And do not argue with the People of the Scripture except in a way that is best, except for those who commit injustice among them, and say, "We believe in that which has been revealed to us and revealed to you. And our God and your God is one; and we are Muslims [in submission] to Him."

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: Do not argue with the People of the Book except only by the best manner, except the unjust among them. Tell them, "We believe in what is revealed to us and to you. Our Lord and your Lord is one. We have submitted ourselves to His will".

46.author: Shakir
46.text: And do not dispute with the followers of the Book except by what is best, except those of them who act unjustly, and say: We believe in that which has been revealed to us and revealed to you, and our Allah and your Allah is One, and to Him do we submit.

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: Believers, argue only in the best way with the People of the Book, [but contend not at all] with such of them as are unjust. Say, "We believe in what has been revealed to us, and what has been revealed to you; our God and your God are one; and to Him we submit."

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: And dispute ye not with the People of the Book, except with means better (than mere disputation), unless it be with those of them who inflict wrong (and injury): but say, "We believe in the revelation which has come down to us and in that which came down to you; Our Allah and your Allah is one; and it is to Him we bow (in Islam)."

46.author: Transliteration
46.text: Wala tujadiloo ahla alkitabi illa biallatee hiya ahsanu illa allatheena thalamoo minhum waqooloo amanna biallathee onzila ilayna waonzila ilaykum wailahuna wailahukum wahidun wanahnu lahu muslimoona

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: That is how We have revealed this Book to you and those to whom We have sent down the Book will believe in it. Only those who are infidels will deny it.

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: And this is how We sent down the Book to you O dear Prophet (Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him); so those to whom We gave the Book believe in it; and some of these (polytheists) accept faith in it; and none except the disbelievers reject Our signs.

47.author: Arberry
47.text: Even so We have sent down to thee the Book. Those to whom We have given the Book believe in it; and some of these believe in it; and none denies Our signs but the unbelievers.

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: And likewise have We sent down unto thee the Book; so those to whom We have vouchsafed the Book believe therein, and of those also some believe therein. And none gainsay Our signs save the infidels.

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: And thus We have sent down the Book (i.e. this Quran) to you (O Muhammad SAW), and those whom We gave the Scripture [the Taurat (Torah) and the Injeel (Gospel) aforetime] believe therein as also do some of these (who are present with you now like 'Abdullah bin Salam) and none but the disbelievers reject Our Ayat [(proofs, signs, verses, lessons, etc., and deny Our Oneness of Lordship and Our Oneness of worship and Our Oneness of Our Names and Qualities: i.e. Islamic Monotheism)].

47.author: Itani
47.text: Likewise, We revealed to you the Scripture. Those to whom We gave the Scripture believe in it, and some of these believe in it. None renounce Our communications except the disbelievers.

47.author: Maududi
47.text: (O Prophet), thus have We bestowed the Book on you. So those on whom We had bestowed the Book before believe in it, and of these (Arabs) too a good many believe in it. It is none but the utter unbelievers who deny Our Signs.

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: And thus We have sent down the Book to you, and those whom We gave the Scripture believe therein as also do some of these and none but the disbelievers reject Our Ayat.

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: In like manner We have revealed unto thee the Scripture, and those unto whom We gave the Scripture aforetime will believe therein; and of these (also) there are some who believe therein. And none deny Our revelations save the disbelievers.

47.author: Qarai
47.text: Thus We have sent down the Book to you; those to whom We have given the Book believe in it, and of these there are some who believe in it, and none contests Our signs except the faithless.

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: As such, We have sent down to you the Book. Those to whom We gave the Book believe in it, and so do some of these. None reject Our verses except the unbelievers.

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: And thus We have sent down to you the Qur'an. And those to whom We [previously] gave the Scripture believe in it. And among these [people of Makkah] are those who believe in it. And none reject Our verses except the disbelievers.

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: We have revealed the Book to you. Some of the People of the Book and some of the pagans also believe in it. No one rejects Our revelations except the infidels.

47.author: Shakir
47.text: And thus have We revealed the Book to you. So those whom We have given the Book believe in it, and of these there are those who believe in it, and none deny Our communications except the unbelievers.

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: Likewise We have sent down the Book to you. Those to whom We gave the scripture believe in it, and so do some of your own people. Only those who deny the truth reject Our revelations.

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: And thus (it is) that We have sent down the Book to thee. So the People of the Book believe therein, as also do some of these (pagan Arabs): and none but Unbelievers reject our signs.

47.author: Transliteration
47.text: Wakathalika anzalna ilayka alkitaba faallatheena ataynahumu alkitaba yuminoona bihi wamin haolai man yuminu bihi wama yajhadu biayatina illa alkafiroona

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: You did not read any Scripture before this, nor wrote one with your right hand, or else these dissemblers would have found a cause to doubt it.

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: And you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) were not reading any Book before it, nor writing with your right hand – if it were, the people of falsehood would surely have doubted.

48.author: Arberry
48.text: Not before this didst thou recite any Book, or inscribe it with thy right hand, for then those who follow falsehood would have doubted.

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: And thou hast not been reciting any book before it, nor hast thou been writing it With thine right hand, for then might the followers of falsehood have doubted.

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: Neither did you (O Muhammad SAW) read any book before it (this Quran), nor did you write any book (whatsoever) with your right hand. In that case, indeed, the followers of falsehood might have doubted.

48.author: Itani
48.text: You did not read any scripture before this, nor did you write it down with your right hand; otherwise the falsifiers would have doubted.

48.author: Maududi
48.text: (O Prophet), you did not recite any Book before, nor did you write it down with your hand; for then the votaries of falsehood would have had a cause for doubt.

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: Neither did you read any book before it nor did you write any book with your right hand. In that case, indeed, the followers of falsehood might have doubted.

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: And thou (O Muhammad) wast not a reader of any scripture before it, nor didst thou write it with thy right hand, for then might those have doubted, who follow falsehood.

48.author: Qarai
48.text: You did not use to recite any scripture before it, nor did you write it with your right hand, for then the impugners would have been skeptical.

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: Never before did you recited any Book, or inscribe it with your right hand. If you had done so, those who follow falsehood would have doubted.

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: And you did not recite before it any scripture, nor did you inscribe one with your right hand. Otherwise the falsifiers would have had [cause for] doubt.

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: You were not able to read or write before the Quran was revealed to you; otherwise, the followers of falsehood would have tried to confuse the matter.

48.author: Shakir
48.text: And you did not recite before it any book, nor did you transcribe one with your right hand, for then could those who say untrue things have doubted.

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: You were not able to read any book before this, nor did you write one down with your hand. If you had done so, the followers of falsehood would have had cause to doubt it.

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: And thou wast not (able) to recite a Book before this (Book came), nor art thou (able) to transcribe it with thy right hand: In that case, indeed, would the talkers of vanities have doubted.

48.author: Transliteration
48.text: Wama kunta tatloo min qablihi min kitabin wala takhuttuhu biyameenika ithan lairtaba almubtiloona

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: In fact, in the minds of those who have intelligence these are clear signs. No one denies Our revelations except those who are unjust.

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: In fact they are clear verses in the hearts of those who have been given knowledge; and none deny Our verses except the unjust.

49.author: Arberry
49.text: Nay; rather it is signs, clear signs in the breasts of those who have been given knowledge; and none denies Our signs but the evildoers.

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: Aye! it in itself manifest signs in the breasts of those who have been vouchsafed knowledge, and none gainsay Our signs save the wrong-doers.

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: Nay, but they, the clear Ayat [i.e. the description and the qualities of Prophet Muhammad SAW written like verses in the Taurat (Torah) and the Injeel (Gospel)] are preserved in the breasts of those who have been given knowledge (from the people of the Scriptures). And none but the Zalimun (polytheists and wrongdoers, etc.) deny Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.).

49.author: Itani
49.text: In fact, it is clear signs in the hearts of those given knowledge. No one renounce Our signs except the unjust.

49.author: Maududi
49.text: But it is a set of Clear Signs in the hearts of those who have been endowed with knowledge. None except the utterly unjust will deny Our Signs.

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: Nay, but it is clear Ayat, (preserved) in the breasts of those who have been given knowledge. And none but the wrongdoers deny Our Ayat.

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: But it is clear revelations in the hearts of those who have been given knowledge, and none deny Our revelations save wrong-doers.

49.author: Qarai
49.text: Indeed, it is [present as] manifest signs in the breasts of those who have been given knowledge, and none contests Our signs except wrongdoers.

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: No, rather they are clear verses in the chests of those who have been given knowledge. None disbelieves Our verses except the harmdoers.

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: Rather, the Qur'an is distinct verses [preserved] within the breasts of those who have been given knowledge. And none reject Our verses except the wrongdoers.

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: In fact, the Quran consists of illustrious verses that exist in the hearts of those who have knowledge. No one rejects Our revelations except the unjust ones.

49.author: Shakir
49.text: Nay! these are clear communications in the breasts of those who are granted knowledge; and none deny Our communications except the unjust.

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: But the Quran is a revelation that is clear to the hearts of those endowed with knowledge. Only the evil-doers refuse to acknowledge Our revelations.

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: Nay, here are Signs self-evident in the hearts of those endowed with knowledge: and none but the unjust reject Our Signs.

49.author: Transliteration
49.text: Bal huwa ayatun bayyinatun fee sudoori allatheena ootoo alAAilma wama yajhadu biayatina illa alththalimoona

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: For they say: "How Is it no signs were sent down to him from his Lord?" Say: "The signs are with God. I am only a warner, plain and simple."

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: And they said, “Why did not signs come down to him from his Lord?” Say, “Signs are only with Allah; and I am purely a Herald of plain warning.”

50.author: Arberry
50.text: They say, 'Why have signs not been gent down upon him from his Lord?' Say: 'The signs are only with God, and I am only a plain warner.

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: And they say: wherefore are not signs sent down upon him from his Lord! Say thou: signs are with Allah only, and I am but a manifest warner.

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: And they say: "Why are not signs sent down to him from his Lord? Say: "The signs are only with Allah, and I am only a plain warner."

50.author: Itani
50.text: And they said, “If only a miracle from his Lord was sent down to him.” Say, “Miracles are only with God, and I am only a clear warner.”

50.author: Maududi
50.text: They say: “Why were Signs from his Lord not sent down upon him?” Say: “The Signs are only with Allah. As for me, I am no more than a plain warner.”

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: And they say: "Why are not signs sent down to him from his Lord" Say: "The signs are only with Allah, and I am only a plain warner."

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: And they say: Why are not portents sent down upon him from his Lord? Say: Portents are with Allah only, and I am but a plain warner.

50.author: Qarai
50.text: They say, ‘Why has not some sign been sent down to him from his Lord?’ Say, ‘These signs are only from Allah, and I am only a manifest warner.’

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: They ask: 'Why has not a sign been sent down upon him from his Lord' Say: 'The signs are only with Allah. I am only a clear warner'

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: But they say, "Why are not signs sent down to him from his Lord?" Say, "The signs are only with Allah, and I am only a clear warner."

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: They say, "Why a miracle is not sent to him from his Lord." Say, "Miracles are in the hands of God. I am simply a warner."

50.author: Shakir
50.text: And they say: Why are not signs sent down upon him from his Lord? Say: The signs are only with Allah, and I am only a plain warner.

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: They say, "Why has no sign been given to him by his Lord?" Say, "The signs are in the hands of God. I am but a plain warner."

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: Ye they say: "Why are not Signs sent down to him from his Lord?" Say: "The signs are indeed with Allah: and I am indeed a clear Warner."

50.author: Transliteration
50.text: Waqaloo lawla onzila AAalayhi ayatun min rabbihi qul innama alayatu AAinda Allahi wainnama ana natheerun mubeenun

51.author: Ahmed Ali
51.text: Is it not sufficient for them that We have revealed the Book to you which is read out to them? It is indeed a grace and reminder for people who believe.

51.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
51.text: Is it not enough for them that We have sent down the Book upon you, which is read to them? Indeed in it are mercy and advice for the Muslims.

51.author: Arberry
51.text: What, is it not sufficient for them that We have sent down upon thee the Book that is recited to them? Surely in that is a mercy, and a reminder to a people who believe.

51.author: Daryabadi
