51.text: Sufficeth it not for them that We have sent down unto thee the Book to be recited unto them? Verily herein is a mercy and an admonition for a people who believe.

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: Is it not sufficient for them that We have sent down to you the Book (the Quran) which is recited to them? Verily, herein is mercy and a reminder (or an admonition) for a people who believe.

51.author: Itani
51.text: Does it not suffice them that We revealed to you the Scripture, which is recited to them? In that is mercy and a reminder for people who believe.

51.author: Maududi
51.text: Does it not suffice for them (as a Sign) that We revealed to you the Book that is recited to them? Surely there is mercy and good counsel in it for those who believe.

51.author: Mubarakpuri
51.text: Is it not sufficient for them that We have sent down to you the Book which is recited to them Verily, herein is mercy and a reminder for a people who believe.

51.author: Pickthall
51.text: Is it not enough for them that We have sent down unto thee the Scripture which is read unto them? Lo! herein verily is mercy, and a reminder for folk who believe.

51.author: Qarai
51.text: Does it not suffice them that We have sent down to you the Book which is recited to them? There is indeed in that a mercy and admonition for a people who have faith.

51.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
51.text: Is it not enough for them that We have sent down to you the Book that is recited to them? Surely, in this there is a mercy and a reminder to a nation who believe.

51.author: Saheeh International
51.text: And is it not sufficient for them that We revealed to you the Book which is recited to them? Indeed in that is a mercy and reminder for a people who believe.

51.author: Sarwar
51.text: Is it not enough for them that We have revealed the Book to you to be recited to them. It is a mercy and a reminder for the believers.

51.author: Shakir
51.text: Is it not enough for them that We have revealed to you the Book which is recited to them? Most surely there is mercy in this and a reminder for a people who believe.

51.author: Wahiduddin Khan
51.text: Is it not sufficient for them that We have sent you down the Book to be recited to them? In this surely there is a blessing and an admonition for a people who believe.

51.author: Yusuf Ali
51.text: And is it not enough for them that we have sent down to thee the Book which is rehearsed to them? Verily, in it is Mercy and a Reminder to those who believe.

51.author: Transliteration
51.text: Awalam yakfihim anna anzalna AAalayka alkitaba yutla AAalayhim inna fee thalika larahmatan wathikra liqawmin yuminoona

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: Say: "God is sufficient as witness between me and you." He knows what is in the heavens and the earth. It is those who believe in falsehood and disbelieve in God who will perish.

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “Allah is Sufficient as a Witness between me and you; He knows all what is in the heavens and in the earth; and those who accepted faith in falsehood and denied faith in Allah – it is they who are the losers.”

52.author: Arberry
52.text: Say: 'God suffices as a witness between me and you.' He knows whatsoever is in the heavens and earth. Those who believe in vanity and disbelieve in God -- those, they are the losers.

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: Say thou: Allah sufficeth for witness between me and you: He knoweth whatsoever is in the heavens and the earth. And they who believe in falsehood and disbelieve in Allah, those: they shall be the losers.

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: Say (to them O Muhammad SAW): "Sufficient is Allah for a witness between me and you. He knows what is in the heavens and on earth." And those who believe in Batil (all false deities other than Allah), and disbelieve in Allah and (in His Oneness), it is they who are the losers.

52.author: Itani
52.text: Say, “God suffices as witness between you and me. He knows everything in the heavens and the Earth. Those who believe in vanity and reject God—it is they who are the losers.”

52.author: Maududi
52.text: Say (O Prophet): “Allah suffices as a witness between me and you. He knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth. As for those who believe in falsehood and are engaged in infidelity with Allah, it is they who will be the losers.”

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: Say: "Sufficient is Allah for a witness between me and you. He knows what is in the heavens and on the earth." And those who believe in falsehood, and disbelieve in Allah, it is they who are the losers.

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: Say (unto them, O Muhammad): Allah sufficeth for witness between me and you. He knoweth whatsoever is in the heavens and the earth. And those who believe in vanity and disbelieve in Allah, they it is who are the losers.

52.author: Qarai
52.text: Say, ‘Allah suffices as a witness between me and you: He knows whatever there is in the heavens and the earth. Those who put faith in falsehood and defy Allah—it is they who are the losers.’

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: Say: 'Allah suffices as a witness between me and you. He knows whatsoever is in the heavens and the earth. Those who believe in falsehood and disbelieve in Allah those are they who are the losers'

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: Say, "Sufficient is Allah between me and you as Witness. He knows what is in the heavens and earth. And they who have believed in falsehood and disbelieved in Allah - it is those who are the losers."

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: Say, "God is sufficient as a witness between me and you. He knows all that is in the heavens and the earth." Those who have faith in falsehood and disbelieve in God are certainly lost.

52.author: Shakir
52.text: Say: Allah is sufficient as a witness between me and you; He knows what is in the heavens and the earth. And (as for) those who believe in the falsehood and disbelieve in Allah, these it is that are the losers.

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: Say, "God is sufficient as a witness between you and me. He knows all that is in the heavens and the earth. Those who believe in falsehood and reject God will surely be the losers."

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: Say: "Enough is Allah for a witness between me and you: He knows what is in the heavens and on earth. And it is those who believe in vanities and reject Allah, that will perish (in the end).

52.author: Transliteration
52.text: Qul kafa biAllahi baynee wabaynakum shaheedan yaAAlamu ma fee alssamawati waalardi waallatheena amanoo bialbatili wakafaroo biAllahi olaika humu alkhasiroona

53.author: Ahmed Ali
53.text: They want you to hasten the punishment: But for a time already determined the punishment would have come upon them. It will come upon them all too suddenly, and they will be caught unawares.

53.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
53.text: And they urge you to hasten the punishment; and if a time had not been fixed, the punishment would have certainly come upon them; and it will indeed come upon them suddenly whilst they are unaware.

53.author: Arberry
53.text: And they demand of thee to hasten the chastisement! But for a stated term the chastisement would have come upon them; but it shall come upon them suddenly, when they are not aware.

53.author: Daryabadi
53.text: And they ask thee to hasten on the torment. And had not there been a term appointed, the torment would surely have come unto them. And surely it shall come upon them of a sudden while they perceive not.

53.author: Hilali & Khan
53.text: And they ask you to hasten on the torment (for them), and had it not been for a term appointed, the torment would certainly have come to them. And surely, it will come upon them suddenly while they perceive not!

53.author: Itani
53.text: And they urge you to hasten the punishment. Were it not for a specified time, the punishment would have come to them. But it will come upon them suddenly, while they are unaware.

53.author: Maududi
53.text: They ask you to hasten in bringing chastisement upon them. Had there not been an appointed term for it, the chastisement would have already visited them; in fact it will come down upon them all of a sudden (at its appointed time) while they will not be aware of it.

53.author: Mubarakpuri
53.text: And they ask you to hasten on the torment, and had it not been for a term appointed, the torment would certainly have come to them. And surely, it will come upon them suddenly while they perceive not!

53.author: Pickthall
53.text: They bid thee hasten on the doom (of Allah). And if a term had not been appointed, the doom would assuredly have come unto them (ere now). And verily it will come upon them suddenly when they perceive not.

53.author: Qarai
53.text: They ask you to hasten the punishment. Yet were it not for a specified time, the punishment would have surely overtaken them. Surely, it will overtake them suddenly while they are unaware.

53.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
53.text: They demand that you hasten the punishment! But had it not been for the stated term, the punishment would come to them; it will come upon them suddenly when they are unaware.

53.author: Saheeh International
53.text: And they urge you to hasten the punishment. And if not for [the decree of] a specified term, punishment would have reached them. But it will surely come to them suddenly while they perceive not.

53.author: Sarwar
53.text: They demand you to bring upon them torment immediately. Had not the time been fixed, the torment would certainly have approached them. It would have come to them suddenly and they would not have even realized how it came.

53.author: Shakir
53.text: And they ask you to hasten on the chastisement; and had not a term been appointed, the chastisement would certainly have come to them; and most certainly it will come to them all of a sudden while they will not perceive.

53.author: Wahiduddin Khan
53.text: They ask you to hasten the punishment. Had there not been an appointed time for it, the punishment would already have come to them. Indeed it will come down upon them suddenly and catch them unawares.

53.author: Yusuf Ali
53.text: They ask thee to hasten on the Punishment (for them): had it not been for a term (of respite) appointed, the Punishment would certainly have come to them: and it will certainly reach them,- of a sudden, while they perceive not!

53.author: Transliteration
53.text: WayastaAAjiloonaka bialAAathabi walawla ajalun musamman lajaahumu alAAathabu walayatiyannahum baghtatan wahum la yashAAuroona

54.author: Ahmed Ali
54.text: They want you to hasten the punishment: Hell will indeed surround the unbelievers.

54.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
54.text: They urge you to hasten the punishment; and indeed hell has encompassed the disbelievers.

54.author: Arberry
54.text: They demand of thee to hasten the chastisement! Lo, Gehenna encompasses the unbelievers.

54.author: Daryabadi
54.text: They ask thee to hasten on the torment; and verily the Hell is about to encompass the infidels.

54.author: Hilali & Khan
54.text: They ask you to hasten on the torment. And verily! Hell, of a surety, will encompass the disbelievers.

54.author: Itani
54.text: They urge you to hasten the punishment. But Hell will engulf the disbelievers.

54.author: Maududi
54.text: They ask you to hasten the chastisement upon them although Hell encompasses the unbelievers.

54.author: Mubarakpuri
54.text: They ask you to hasten on the torment. And verily, Hell, of a surety, will encompass the disbelievers.

54.author: Pickthall
54.text: They bid thee hasten on the doom, when lo! hell verily will encompass the disbelievers

54.author: Qarai
54.text: They ask you to hasten the punishment, and indeed hell will besiege the faithless

54.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
54.text: They demand that you hasten on the punishment! Gehenna (Hell) will encompass the unbelievers.

54.author: Saheeh International
54.text: They urge you to hasten the punishment. And indeed, Hell will be encompassing of the disbelievers

54.author: Sarwar
54.text: They demand you to bring upon them the torment immediately. Hell will certainly engulf the unbelievers.

54.author: Shakir
54.text: They ask you to hasten on the chastisement, and most surely hell encompasses the unbelievers;

54.author: Wahiduddin Khan
54.text: They ask you to hasten the punishment, but surely, Hell is [already] encompassing those who deny the truth.

54.author: Yusuf Ali
54.text: They ask thee to hasten on the Punishment: but, of a surety, Hell will encompass the Rejecters of Faith!-

54.author: Transliteration
54.text: YastaAAjiloonaka bialAAathabi wainna jahannama lamuheetatun bialkafireena

55.author: Ahmed Ali
55.text: The day the punishment comes upon them from above and underneath their feet, (God will) say: "And now taste of what you had done."

55.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
55.text: The day when the punishment will envelop them from above them and from below their feet, and He will say, “Taste the flavour of your deeds!”

55.author: Arberry
55.text: Upon the day the chastisement shall overwhelm them from above them and from under their feet, and He shall say, 'Taste now what you were doing!'

55.author: Daryabadi
55.text: On the Day whereon the torment shall cover them from above them and from underneath their feet, and He shall say: taste that which ye have been working!

55.author: Hilali & Khan
55.text: On the Day when the torment (Hell-fire) shall cover them from above them and from underneath their feet, and it will be said: "Taste what you used to do."

55.author: Itani
55.text: On the Day when the punishment will envelop them, from above them, and from beneath their feet, He will say, “Taste what you used to do!”

55.author: Maududi
55.text: (They will become aware of it) the Day when the chastisement will overwhelm them from above and from under their feet, and He will say to them: “Taste now the consequence of the deeds that you used to commit.”

55.author: Mubarakpuri
55.text: On the Day when the torment shall cover them from above them and from beneath their feet, and it will be said: "Taste what you used to do."

55.author: Pickthall
55.text: On the day when the doom will overwhelm them from above them and from underneath their feet, and He will say: Taste what ye used to do!

55.author: Qarai
55.text: on the day when the punishment envelopes them, from above them and from under their feet, and He will say, ‘Taste what you used to do!’

55.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
55.text: On the Day the punishment shall cover them from above them and from beneath their feet, He shall say: 'Taste now what you were doing'

55.author: Saheeh International
55.text: On the Day the punishment will cover them from above them and from below their feet and it is said, "Taste [the result of] what you used to do."

55.author: Sarwar
55.text: They will be told on the Day of Judgment, when the torment will surround them from all sides, "Suffer the consequences of your deeds."

55.author: Shakir
55.text: On the day when the chastisement shall cover them from above them, and from beneath their feet; and He shall say: Taste what you did.

55.author: Wahiduddin Khan
55.text: On the Day the punishment envelops them from above them and from underneath their feet, they will be told, "Taste [the punishment] for what you used to do!"

55.author: Yusuf Ali
55.text: On the Day that the Punishment shall cover them from above them and from below them, and (a Voice) shall say: "Taste ye (the fruits) of your deeds!"

55.author: Transliteration
55.text: Yawma yaghshahumu alAAathabu min fawqihim wamin tahti arjulihim wayaqoolu thooqoo ma kuntum taAAmaloona

56.author: Ahmed Ali
56.text: O My creatures who believe, surely My earth has plenty of scope and so worship only Me.

56.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
56.text: O My bondmen who believe! Indeed My earth is spacious – therefore worship Me alone.

56.author: Arberry
56.text: O My servants who believe, surely My earth is wide; therefore Me do you serve!

56.author: Daryabadi
56.text: My bondmen, who believe! verily My earth is wide, so Me alone worship.

56.author: Hilali & Khan
56.text: O My slaves who believe! Certainly, spacious is My earth. Therefore worship Me (Alone)."

56.author: Itani
56.text: O My servants who have believed: My earth is vast, so worship Me alone.

56.author: Maududi
56.text: O My servants who believe, verily My earth is vast; so serve Me alone.

56.author: Mubarakpuri
56.text: O My servants who believe! Certainly, spacious is My earth. Therefore worship Me.

56.author: Pickthall
56.text: O my bondmen who believe! Lo! My earth is spacious. Therefor serve Me only.

56.author: Qarai
56.text: O My servants who have faith! My earth is indeed vast. So worship [only] Me.

56.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
56.text: O My worshipers who believe, My earth is vast, therefore worship Me!

56.author: Saheeh International
56.text: O My servants who have believed, indeed My earth is spacious, so worship only Me.

56.author: Sarwar
56.text: My believing servants, My land is vast. Worship Me alone.

56.author: Shakir
56.text: O My servants who believe! surely My earth is vast, therefore Me alone should you serve.

56.author: Wahiduddin Khan
56.text: My servants who believe, My earth is vast, so worship Me alone.

56.author: Yusuf Ali
56.text: O My servants who believe! truly, spacious is My Earth: therefore serve ye Me - (and Me alone)!

56.author: Transliteration
56.text: Ya AAibadiya allatheena amanoo inna ardee wasiAAatun faiyyaya faoAAbudooni

57.author: Ahmed Ali
57.text: Every soul has to know the taste of death. You will then be sent back to Us.

57.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
57.text: Every soul must taste death; then it is to Us that you will return.

57.author: Arberry
57.text: Every soul shall taste of death; then unto Us you shall be returned.

57.author: Daryabadi
57.text: Every soul shall taste of death; then unto Us ye shall be returned.

57.author: Hilali & Khan
57.text: Everyone shall taste the death. Then unto Us you shall be returned.

57.author: Itani
57.text: Every soul will taste death. Then to Us you will be returned.

57.author: Maududi
57.text: Every being shall taste death, then it is to Us that you shall be sent back.

57.author: Mubarakpuri
57.text: Everyone shall taste death. Then unto Us you shall be returned.

57.author: Pickthall
57.text: Every soul will taste of death. Then unto Us ye will be returned.

57.author: Qarai
57.text: Every soul shall taste death. Then you shall be brought back to Us.

57.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
57.text: Every soul shall taste death, and to Us you shall be returned.

57.author: Saheeh International
57.text: Every soul will taste death. Then to Us will you be returned.

57.author: Sarwar
57.text: Every soul will experience the agony of death and to Me you will all return.

57.author: Shakir
57.text: Every soul must taste of death, then to Us you shall be brought back.

57.author: Wahiduddin Khan
57.text: Every soul shall taste death and then to Us you shall return.

57.author: Yusuf Ali
57.text: Every soul shall have a taste of death in the end to Us shall ye be brought back.

57.author: Transliteration
57.text: Kullu nafsin thaiqatu almawti thumma ilayna turjaAAoona

58.author: Ahmed Ali
58.text: We shall admit those who believe and do the right to empyreal gardens with rivers rippling by, where they will abide for ever. How excellent the guerdon of those who toil,

58.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
58.text: And those who believed and did good deeds – indeed We will, surely, place them on high positions in Paradise beneath which rivers flow, abiding in it for ever; what an excellent reward of the performers!

58.author: Arberry
58.text: And those who believe, and do righteous deeds, We shall surely lodge them in lofty chambers of paradise, underneath which rivers flow, therein dwelling forever; and excellent is the wage of those who labour,

58.author: Daryabadi
58.text: And those who believe and work righteous works--them We shall surely settle in lofty dwellins of the; they shall be of the Garden whereunder the rivers flow; they shall be abiders therein. Excellent is the hire of the Workers.

58.author: Hilali & Khan
58.text: And those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds, to them We shall surely give lofty dwellings in Paradise, underneath which rivers flow, to live therein forever. Excellent is the reward of the workers.

58.author: Itani
58.text: Those who believe and work righteousness—We will settle them in Paradise, in mansions under which rivers flow, dwelling therein forever. Excellent is the compensation for the workers.

58.author: Maududi
58.text: We shall house those who believed and acted righteously in the lofty mansions of Paradise beneath which rivers flow. There they shall remain for ever. How excellent a reward it is for those who acted (in obedience to Allah),

58.author: Mubarakpuri
58.text: And those who believe and do righteous good deeds, to them We shall surely give lofty dwellings in Paradise, beneath which rivers flow, to live therein forever. Excellent is the reward for the workers.

58.author: Pickthall
58.text: Those who believe and do good works, them verily We shall house in lofty dwellings of the Garden underneath which rivers flow. There they will dwell secure. How sweet the guerdon of the toilers,

58.author: Qarai
58.text: Those who have faith and do righteous deeds, We will surely settle them in the lofty abodes of paradise, with streams running in them, to remain in them [forever]. How excellent is the reward of the workers!

58.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
58.text: Those who believe and do good deeds We shall lodge them in the rooms of Paradise underneath which rivers flow, therein living for ever, an excellent wage for those who labor,

58.author: Saheeh International
58.text: And those who have believed and done righteous deeds - We will surely assign to them of Paradise [elevated] chambers beneath which rivers flow, wherein they abide eternally. Excellent is the reward of the [righteous] workers

58.author: Sarwar
58.text: We shall give mansions in Paradise wherein streams flow to the righteously striving believers and therein they will live forever. How blessed is the reward of the hard working people

58.author: Shakir
58.text: And (as for) those who believe and do good, We will certainly give them abode in the high places in gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein; how good the reward of the workers:

58.author: Wahiduddin Khan
58.text: We shall lodge forever those who believe and do good works in the mansions of Paradise beside which rivers flow. How excellent is the reward of those who labour,

58.author: Yusuf Ali
58.text: But those who believe and work deeds of righteousness - to them shall We give a Home in Heaven,- lofty mansions beneath which flow rivers,- to dwell therein for aye;- an excellent reward for those who do (good)!-

58.author: Transliteration
58.text: Waallatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati lanubawwiannahum mina aljannati ghurafan tajree min tahtiha alanharu khalideena feeha niAAma ajru alAAamileena

59.author: Ahmed Ali
59.text: Who persevere and place their trust in their Lord.

59.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
59.text: Those who patiently endured and who rely only upon their Lord.

59.author: Arberry
59.text: such men as are patient, and put their trust in their Lord.

59.author: Daryabadi
59.text: Who persevere and in their Lord trust!

59.author: Hilali & Khan
59.text: Those who are patient, and put their trust (only) in their Lord (Allah).

59.author: Itani
59.text: Those who endure patiently, and in their Lord they trust.

59.author: Maududi
59.text: who remained steadfast and put their trust in their Lord!

59.author: Mubarakpuri
59.text: Those who are patient, and put their trust in their Lord.

59.author: Pickthall
59.text: Who persevere, and put their trust in their Lord!

59.author: Qarai
59.text: —Those who are patient and who put their trust in their Lord.

59.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
59.text: those who are patient, and put their trust in their Lord.

59.author: Saheeh International
59.text: Who have been patient and upon their Lord rely.

59.author: Sarwar
59.text: who have exercised patience and who have had trust in their Lord.

59.author: Shakir
59.text: Those who are patient, and on their Lord do they rely.

59.author: Wahiduddin Khan
59.text: and who are steadfast and put their trust in their Lord.

59.author: Yusuf Ali
59.text: Those who persevere in patience, and put their trust, in their Lord and Cherisher.

60.author: Ahmed Ali
60.text: How many living things there are on the earth that do not Store their food; God provides them as well as you. He is all-hearing and all-knowing.

60.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
60.text: And many creatures walk upon the earth that do not carry their own sustenance; Allah provides the sustenance to them and to you; and only He is the All Hearing, the All Knowing.

60.author: Arberry
60.text: How many a beast that bears not its own provision, but God provides for it and you! He is the All-hearer, the All-knower.

60.author: Daryabadi
60.text: And how many a moving creature there is that beareth not its provision! Allah provideth for it and for you. And He is the Hearer, the Knower.

60.author: Hilali & Khan
60.text: And so many a moving (living) creature there is, that carries not its own provision! Allah provides for it and for you. And He is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower.

60.author: Itani
60.text: How many a creature there is that does not carry its provision? God provides for them, and for you. He is the Hearer, the Knowledgeable.

60.author: Maududi
60.text: How many an animal there is that does not carry about its sustenance. Allah provides sustenance to them and to you. He is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

60.author: Mubarakpuri
60.text: And so many a moving creature carries not its own provision! Allah provides for it and for you. And He is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower.

60.author: Pickthall
60.text: And how many an animal there is that beareth not its own provision! Allah provideth for it and for you. He is the Hearer, the Knower.

60.author: Qarai
60.text: How many an animal there is that does not carry its own provision. Allah provides them and you, and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.

60.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
60.text: How many a beast does not bear its own provision. Allah provides for it, as (He provides) for you. He is the Hearer, the Knower.

60.author: Saheeh International
60.text: And how many a creature carries not its [own] provision. Allah provides for it and for you. And He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

60.author: Sarwar
60.text: There are many living creatures which do not carry their sustenance, but God provides them and you with sustenance. He is All-hearing and All-knowing.

60.author: Shakir
60.text: And how many a living creature that does not carry its sustenance: Allah sustains it and yourselves; and He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

60.author: Wahiduddin Khan
60.text: How many creatures cannot fend for themselves! God provides for them and for you. He is the All Hearing, the All Knowing.

60.author: Yusuf Ali
60.text: How many are the creatures that carry not their own sustenance? It is Allah who feeds (both) them and you: for He hears and knows (all things).

60.author: Transliteration
60.text: Wakaayyin min dabbatin la tahmilu rizqaha Allahu yarzuquha waiyyakum wahuwa alssameeAAu alAAaleemu

61.author: Ahmed Ali
61.text: If you ask them: "Who created the heavens and the earth, and who set the sun and the moon to work?" They will answer: "God." Why then do they vacillate?

61.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
61.text: And if you ask them, “Who created the heavens and the earth, and subjected the sun and the moon?”, they will surely say, “Allah”; so where are they reverting?

61.author: Arberry
61.text: If thou askest them, 'Who created the heavens and the earth and subjected the sun and the moon?' they will say, 'God.' How then are they perverted?

61.author: Daryabadi
61.text: And wert thou to ask them.: who hath created the heavens and the earth and subjected the sun and the moon they would surely say: Allah. How then are they deviating?

61.author: Hilali & Khan
61.text: If you were to ask them: "Who has created the heavens and the earth and subjected the sun and the moon?" They will surely reply: "Allah." How then are they deviating (as polytheists and disbelievers)?

61.author: Itani
61.text: And if you asked them, “Who created the heavens and the earth and regulated the sun and the moon?” They would say, “God.” Why then do they deviate?

61.author: Maududi
61.text: If you were to ask them: “Who created the heavens and the earth and Who has kept the sun and the moon in subjection?” they will certainly say: “Allah.” How come, then, they are being deluded from the Truth?

61.author: Mubarakpuri
61.text: And if you were to ask them: "Who has created the heavens and the earth and subjected the sun and the moon" They will surely reply: "Allah." How then are they deviating

61.author: Pickthall
61.text: And if thou wert to ask them: Who created the heavens and the earth, and constrained the sun and the moon (to their appointed work)? they would say: Allah. How then are they turned away?

61.author: Qarai
61.text: If you ask them, ‘Who created the heavens and the earth, and who has disposed the sun and the moon?’ They will surely say, ‘Allah.’ Then where do they stray?

61.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
61.text: If you ask them: 'Who has created the heavens and the earth and subjected the sun and the moon' They will say: 'Allah' How perverted they are!

61.author: Saheeh International
61.text: If you asked them, "Who created the heavens and earth and subjected the sun and the moon?" they would surely say, "Allah." Then how are they deluded?

61.author: Sarwar
61.text: If you ask them, "Who has created the heavens and the earth and has subdued the sun and moon?" They will say, "God has done it." So why are they wandering about!?

61.author: Shakir
61.text: And if you ask them, Who created the heavens and the earth and made the sun and the moon subservient, they will certainly say, Allah. Whence are they then turned away?

61.author: Wahiduddin Khan
61.text: If you ask them who it is that has created the heavens and the earth and subjugated the sun and the moon, they will say, "God." How then are they turned away?

61.author: Yusuf Ali
61.text: If indeed thou ask them who has created the heavens and the earth and subjected the sun and the moon (to his Law), they will certainly reply, "Allah". How are they then deluded away (from the truth)?

61.author: Transliteration
61.text: Walain saaltahum man khalaqa alssamawati waalarda wasakhkhara alshshamsa waalqamara layaqoolunna Allahu faanna yufakoona

62.author: Ahmed Ali
62.text: God increases the means of those of His creatures as He please, or limits them for whomsoever He will. He is certainly cognisant of everything.

62.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
62.text: Allah eases the sustenance for whomever He wills among His bondmen, and restricts it for whomever He wills; indeed Allah knows all things.

62.author: Arberry
62.text: God outspreads and straitens His provision to whomsoever He will of His servants; God has knowledge of everything.

62.author: Daryabadi
62.text: Allah expandeth provision for whomsoever He Will of His bondmen and stinteth it for him. Verily Allah is of everything the Knower.

62.author: Hilali & Khan
62.text: Allah enlarges the provision for whom He wills of His slaves, and straitens it for whom (He wills). Verily, Allah is the All-Knower of everything.

62.author: Itani
62.text: God expands the provision for whomever He wills of His servants, and restricts it. God is Cognizant of all things.

62.author: Maududi
62.text: Allah enlarges the sustenance of any of His servants whom He will, and straitens the sustenance of whom He will. Surely Allah has knowledge of everything.

62.author: Mubarakpuri
62.text: Allah expands the provision for whom He wills of His servants, and straitens it for whom (He wills). Verily, Allah is the All-Knower of everything.

62.author: Pickthall
62.text: Allah maketh the provision wide for whom He will of His bondmen, and straiteneth it for whom (He will). Lo! Allah is Aware of all things.

62.author: Qarai
62.text: Allah expands the provision for whomever He wishes of His servants, and tightens it for him. Indeed Allah has knowledge of all things.

62.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
62.text: Allah outspreads and restricts His provision to whomsoever He will of His worshipers. Allah has knowledge of all things.

62.author: Saheeh International
62.text: Allah extends provision for whom He wills of His servants and restricts for him. Indeed Allah is, of all things, Knowing.

62.author: Sarwar
62.text: God increases the sustenance of whichever of His servants He wants and He determines their share. God has the knowledge of all things.

62.author: Shakir
62.text: Allah makes abundant the means of subsistence for whom He pleases of His servants, and straitens them for whom (He pleases) surely Allah is Cognizant of all things.

62.author: Wahiduddin Khan
62.text: God gives abundantly to whom He will and sparingly to whom He pleases. God has full knowledge of all things.

62.author: Yusuf Ali
62.text: Allah enlarges the sustenance (which He gives) to whichever of His servants He pleases; and He (similarly) grants by (strict) measure, (as He pleases): for Allah has full knowledge of all things.

62.author: Transliteration
62.text: Allahu yabsutu alrrizqa liman yashao min AAibadihi wayaqdiru lahu inna Allaha bikulli shayin AAaleemun

63.author: Ahmed Ali
63.text: If you ask them: "Who sends down rain from the sky and quickens the earth when it is dead?" They will answer: "God." Say: "All praise be to God." But most of them do not understand.

63.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
63.text: And if you ask them, “Who sent down the water from the sky, and with it revived the earth after its death?”, they will surely say, “Allah”; proclaim, “All praise is to Allah”; in fact most of them do not have any sense.

63.author: Arberry
63.text: If thou askest them, 'Who sends down out of heaven water, and therewith revives the earth after it is dead?' they will say, 'God.' Say: 'Praise belongs to God.' Nay, but most of them have no understanding.

63.author: Daryabadi
63.text: And wert thou to ask them: who sendeth down water from the heaven, and therewith quickeneth the earth after the death thereof? they would surely say: Allah. Say thou: all praise unto Allah! Aye! most of them reflect not.

63.author: Hilali & Khan
63.text: If you were to ask them: "Who sends down water (rain) from the sky, and gives life therewith to the earth after its death?" They will surely reply: "Allah." Say: "All the praises and thanks be to Allah!" Nay! Most of them have no sense.

63.author: Itani
63.text: And if you asked them, “Who sends water down from the sky, with which He revives the earth after it had died?” They would say, “God.” Say, “Praise be to God.” But most of them do not understand.

63.author: Maududi
63.text: If you were to ask them: “Who sent down water from the sky and therewith revived the earth after its death?” they will certainly say: “Allah.” Say: “To Allah alone be praise and thanks.” But most people do not understand.

63.author: Mubarakpuri
63.text: And if you were to ask them: "Who sends down water from the sky, and gives life therewith to the earth after its death" They will surely reply: "Allah." Say: "All the praises and thanks be to Allah!" Nay, most of them have no sense.

63.author: Pickthall
63.text: And if thou wert to ask them: Who causeth water to come down from the sky, and therewith reviveth the earth after its death? they verily would say: Allah. Say: Praise be to Allah! But most of them have no sense.

63.author: Qarai
63.text: And if you ask them, ‘Who sends down water from the sky, with which He revives the earth after its death?’ They will surely say, ‘Allah.’ Say, ‘All praise belongs to Allah!’ But most of them do not exercise their reason.

63.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
63.text: If you were to ask them: ''Who sends down water out of the sky and thereby revives the earth after it was dead' they will reply: 'Allah' Say: 'Praise, belongs to Allah' No, but most of them do not understand.

63.author: Saheeh International
63.text: And if you asked them, "Who sends down rain from the sky and gives life thereby to the earth after its lifelessness?" they would surely say " Allah." Say, "Praise to Allah "; but most of them do not reason.

63.author: Sarwar
63.text: If you ask them, "Who has sent down water from the sky to revive the dead earth?" They will say, "God has done it." Say, "It is only God who deserves all praise, but many of them do not understand."

63.author: Shakir
63.text: And if you ask them Who is it that sends down water from the clouds, then gives life to the earth with it after its death, they will certainly say, Allah. Say: All praise is due to Allah. Nay, most of them do not understand.

63.author: Wahiduddin Khan
63.text: And if you ask them who it is that sends down water from the sky and revives the earth with it after its death, they will surely answer, "God." Then praise be to God. But most of them do not understand.

63.author: Yusuf Ali
63.text: And if indeed thou ask them who it is that sends down rain from the sky, and gives life therewith to the earth after its death, they will certainly reply, "Allah!" Say, "Praise be to Allah!" But most of them understand not.

63.author: Transliteration
63.text: Walain saaltahum man nazzala mina alssamai maan faahya bihi alarda min baAAdi mawtiha layaqoolunna Allahu quli alhamdu lillahi bal aktharuhum la yaAAqiloona

64.author: Ahmed Ali
64.text: The life of this world is only a sport and play. It is surely the home of the Hereafter that will indeed be life extended and new, if only they knew!

64.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
64.text: And this worldly life is nothing but pastime and game; indeed the abode of the Hereafter – that is indeed the true life; if only they knew.

64.author: Arberry
64.text: This present life is naught but a diversion and a sport; surely the Last Abode is Life, did they but know.

64.author: Daryabadi
64.text: And this life of the world is but: sport and play. Verily the home of the Hereafter--that is life indeed, if they but knew!

64.author: Hilali & Khan
64.text: And this life of the world is only amusement and play! Verily, the home of the Hereafter, that is the life indeed (i.e. the eternal life that will never end), if they but knew

64.author: Itani
64.text: The life of this world is nothing but diversion and play, and the Home of the Hereafter is the Life, if they only knew.

64.author: Maududi
64.text: The present life is nothing but sport and amusement. The true life is in the Abode of the Hereafter; if only they knew.

64.author: Mubarakpuri
64.text: And this life of the world is only an amusement and a play! Verily, the home of the Hereafter -- that is the life indeed, if they but knew.

64.author: Pickthall
64.text: This life of the world is but a pastime and a game. Lo! the home of the Hereafter - that is Life, if they but knew.

64.author: Qarai
64.text: The life of this world is nothing but diversion and play, but the abode of the Hereafter is indeed Life (itself), had they known!

64.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
64.text: The life of this world is nothing but a diversion and play. Indeed, the Everlasting Residence is the Eternal Life, if they but knew.

64.author: Saheeh International
64.text: And this worldly life is not but diversion and amusement. And indeed, the home of the Hereafter - that is the [eternal] life, if only they knew.

64.author: Sarwar
64.text: The worldly life is not more than a childish game. It is the life hereafter which will be the real life, if only they knew it.

64.author: Shakir
64.text: And this life of the world is nothing but a sport and a play; and as for the next abode, that most surely is the life-- did they but know!

64.author: Wahiduddin Khan
64.text: The life of this world is nothing but sport and a diversion. It is the life of the Hereafter which is the only true life, if they but knew it.

64.author: Yusuf Ali
64.text: What is the life of this world but amusement and play? but verily the Home in the Hereafter,- that is life indeed, if they but knew.

64.author: Transliteration
64.text: Wama hathihi alhayatu alddunya illa lahwun walaAAibun wainna alddara alakhirata lahiya alhayawanu law kanoo yaAAlamoona

65.author: Ahmed Ali
65.text: When they board a ship they call on God, placing their faith wholly in Him. But when He brings them safely back to shore, they begin to associate others with Him,

65.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
65.text: And when they board the ships they pray to Allah, believing purely in Him; so when He delivers them safely to land, they immediately begin ascribing partners (to Him)!

65.author: Arberry
65.text: When they embark in the ships, they call on God, making their religion sincerely His; but when He has delivered them to the land, they associate others with Him,

65.author: Daryabadi
65.text: So When they mount upon the ships they call on Allah making pure for Him the religion, then when He delivereth them safely on the land, lo! they associate

65.author: Hilali & Khan
65.text: And when they embark on a ship, they invoke Allah, making their Faith pure for Him only, but when He brings them safely to land, behold, they give a share of their worship to others.

65.author: Itani
65.text: When they embark on a vessel, they pray to God, devoting their faith to Him; but once He has delivered them safely to land, they attribute partners to Him.

65.author: Maududi
65.text: When they embark in the ships they call upon Allah, consecrating their faith to Him. But when He rescues them and brings them to land, they suddenly begin to associate others with Allah in His Divinity

65.author: Mubarakpuri
65.text: And when they embark on a ship, they invoke Allah, making their faith pure for Him only, but when He brings them safely to land, behold, they give a share of their worship to others.

65.author: Pickthall
65.text: And when they mount upon the ships they pray to Allah, making their faith pure for Him only, but when He bringeth them safe to land, behold! they ascribe partners (unto Him),

65.author: Qarai
65.text: When they board the ship, they invoke Allah putting exclusive faith in Him, but when He delivers them to land, behold, they ascribe partners [to Him],

65.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
65.text: When they embark upon ships, they call to Allah making their religion sincerely His; but when He brings them safe to the land, they associate others with Him

65.author: Saheeh International
65.text: And when they board a ship, they supplicate Allah, sincere to Him in religion. But when He delivers them to the land, at once they associate others with Him

65.author: Sarwar
65.text: When they sail in a boat, they sincerely pray to God with pure faith. But when We bring them safely to land, they start considering things equal to God.

65.author: Shakir
65.text: So when they ride in the ships they call upon Allah, being sincerely obedient to Him, but when He brings them safe to the land, lo! they associate others (with Him);

65.author: Wahiduddin Khan
65.text: When they board a vessel, they call on God, sincere in their faith for Him alone; but when He brings them safe to land, they begin to ascribe partners to Him.

65.author: Yusuf Ali
65.text: Now, if they embark on a boat, they call on Allah, making their devotion sincerely (and exclusively) to Him; but when He has delivered them safely to (dry) land, behold, they give a share (of their worship to others)!-

65.author: Transliteration
65.text: Faitha rakiboo fee alfulki daAAawoo Allaha mukhliseena lahu alddeena falamma najjahum ila albarri itha hum yushrikoona

66.author: Ahmed Ali
66.text: And deny what We had given them, in order to go on enjoying themselves. They will come to know soon.

66.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
66.text: In order to be ungrateful for Our favours; and to use the comforts; therefore they will soon come to know.

66.author: Arberry
66.text: that they may be ungrateful for what We have given them, and take their enjoyment; they will soon know!

66.author: Daryabadi
66.text: So that they become ingrate for that which We have vouchsafed unto them, and that they enjoy themselves; but presently they shall know.

66.author: Hilali & Khan
66.text: So that they become ingrate for that which We have given them, and that they take their enjoyment (as a warning and a threat), but they will come to know.

66.author: Itani
66.text: To be ungrateful for what We have given them, and to enjoy themselves. They will surely come to know.

66.author: Maududi
66.text: that they may be ungrateful for the rescue that We granted them, and that they may revel in the pleasures (of the present life). Soon they shall come to know.

66.author: Mubarakpuri
66.text: So that they become ingrate for that which We have given them, and that they take their enjoyment, but they will come to know.

66.author: Pickthall
66.text: That they may disbelieve in that which We have given them, and that they may take their ease. But they will come to know.

66.author: Qarai
66.text: being ungrateful for what We have given them! Let them enjoy. Soon they will know!

66.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
66.text: showing ingratitude for what We have given them and take their enjoyment; soon they shall know!

66.author: Saheeh International
66.text: So that they will deny what We have granted them, and they will enjoy themselves. But they are going to know.

66.author: Sarwar
66.text: Let them be ungrateful to what We have granted them. Let them enjoy themselves, but they will soon know (the consequences of their deeds).

66.author: Shakir
66.text: Thus they become ungrateful for what We have given them, so that they may enjoy; but they shall soon know.

66.author: Wahiduddin Khan
66.text: And thus they may show utter ingratitude for Our favours; let them enjoy themselves for a time. But they will soon come to know.

66.author: Yusuf Ali
66.text: Disdaining ungratefully Our gifts, and giving themselves up to (worldly) enjoyment! But soon will they know.

66.author: Transliteration
66.text: Liyakfuroo bima ataynahum waliyatamattaAAoo fasawfa yaAAlamoona

67.author: Ahmed Ali
67.text: Do they not see that We have given them a safe sanctuary, while all around them men are being despoiled? Do they then believe what is false, and deny the bounty of God?

67.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
67.text: Did they not observe that We have created a refuge in the Sacred Land, and people around them are snatched away? So do they believe in falsehood and are ungrateful for the favours of Allah?

67.author: Arberry
67.text: Have they not seen that We have appointed a sanctuary secure, while all about them the people are snatched away? What, do they believe in vanity, and do they disbelieve in God's blessing?

67.author: Daryabadi
67.text: Behold they not that We have appointed an inviolable sanctuary, while men are being snatched away round about them? in falsehood then believe they, and unto the favour of Allah will they be ingrate?

67.author: Hilali & Khan
67.text: Have they not seen that We have made (Makkah) a sanctuary secure, and that men are being snatched away from all around them? Then do they believe in Batil (falsehood - polytheism, idols and all deities other than Allah), and deny (become ingrate for) the Graces of Allah?

67.author: Itani
67.text: Do they not see that We established a Secure Sanctuary, while all around them the people are being carried away? Do they believe in falsehood, and reject the blessings of God?

67.author: Maududi
67.text: Do they not see that We have given them a sanctuary of safety whereas people around them are being snatched away? So, do they believe in falsehood and ungratefully deny Allah's bounties?

67.author: Mubarakpuri
67.text: Have they not seen that We have made a secure sanctuary, while men are being snatched away from all around them Then do they believe in falsehood, and deny the graces of Allah

67.author: Pickthall
67.text: Have they not seen that We have appointed a sanctuary immune (from violence), while mankind are ravaged all around them? Do they then believe in falsehood and disbelieve in the bounty of Allah?

67.author: Qarai
67.text: Have they not seen that We have appointed a safe sanctuary, while the people are despoiled all around them? Would they then believe in falsehood and be ungrateful toward the blessing of Allah?

67.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
67.text: Do they not see how We appointed a safe Sanctuary while all around them people are snatched away? Would they believe in falsehood and do they disbelieve in the favor of Allah!

67.author: Saheeh International
67.text: Have they not seen that We made [Makkah] a safe sanctuary, while people are being taken away all around them? Then in falsehood do they believe, and in the favor of Allah they disbelieve?

67.author: Sarwar
67.text: Have the (pagans) not seen that We have made the holy precinct a safe place while all the people around are suffering terror. Do they believe in falsehood and disbelieve in God's bounties?

67.author: Shakir
67.text: Do they not see that We have made a sacred territory secure, while men are carried off by force from around them? Will they still believe in the falsehood and disbelieve in the favour of Allah?

67.author: Wahiduddin Khan
67.text: Have they not seen how We have granted them a safe sanctuary, though all around them people are snatched away? Would they still believe in falsehood, and deny the favour of God?

67.author: Yusuf Ali
67.text: Do they not then see that We have made a sanctuary secure, and that men are being snatched away from all around them? Then, do they believe in that which is vain, and reject the Grace of Allah?

67.author: Transliteration
67.text: Awalam yaraw anna jaAAalna haraman aminan wayutakhattafu alnnasu min hawlihim afabialbatili yuminoona wabiniAAmati Allahi yakfuroona

68.author: Ahmed Ali
68.text: Who is more unjust than he who fabricates a lie about God, or denies the truth when it has come to him? Is there not an abode for unbelievers in Hell?

68.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
68.text: And who is more unjust than one who fabricates lies against Allah, or denies the truth* when it comes to him? Is there not a place in hell for disbelievers? (* The Holy Prophet and / or the Holy Qur’an).

68.author: Arberry
68.text: And who does greater evil than he who forges against God a lie, or cries lies to the truth when it comes to him? What, is there not in Gehenna a lodging for the unbelievers?

68.author: Daryabadi
68.text: And who is a greater wrong-doer than he who fabricateth a lie against Allah or belieth the truth when it cometh unto him Will there not be in the Hell an abiding-place for the infidels?

68.author: Hilali & Khan
68.text: And who does more wrong than he who invents a lie against Allah or denies the truth (Muhammad SAW and his doctrine of Islamic Monotheism and this Quran), when it comes to him? Is there not a dwelling in Hell for disbelievers (in the Oneness of Allah and in His Messenger Muhammad SAW)?

68.author: Itani
68.text: And who does greater wrong than he who fabricates lies and attributes them to God, or calls the truth a lie when it has come to him? Is there not in Hell a dwelling for the blasphemers?

68.author: Maududi
68.text: Who can be more unjust than he who foists a lie on Allah or gives the lie to the Truth after it has come to him? Is Hell not the resort of the unbelievers?

68.author: Mubarakpuri
68.text: And who does more wrong than he who invents a lie against Allah or denies the truth, when it comes to him Is there not a dwelling in Hell for the disbelievers

68.author: Pickthall
68.text: Who doeth greater wrong than he who inventeth a lie concerning Allah, or denieth the truth when it cometh unto him? Is not there a home in hell for disbelievers?

68.author: Qarai
68.text: Who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah, or denies the truth when it comes to him? Is not the [final] abode of the faithless in hell?

68.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
68.text: And who does greater evil that he who forges a lie against Allah or belied the truth when it comes to him! Is there not a lodging in Gehenna for the unbelievers?

68.author: Saheeh International
68.text: And who is more unjust than one who invents a lie about Allah or denies the truth when it has come to him? Is there not in Hell a [sufficient] residence for the disbelievers?

68.author: Sarwar
68.text: Who is more unjust than one who invents falsehood against God or rejects the Truth after it has come to him? Is not hell the dwelling for the disbelievers.

68.author: Shakir
68.text: And who is more unjust than one who forges a lie against Allah, or gives the lie to the truth when it has come to him? Will not in hell be the abode of the unbelievers?

68.author: Wahiduddin Khan
68.text: Who does greater wrong than he who invents a lie about God or rejects the truth when it comes to him? Is Hell not the home for those who deny the truth.

68.author: Yusuf Ali
68.text: And who does more wrong than he who invents a lie against Allah or rejects the Truth when it reaches him? Is there not a home in Hell for those who reject Faith?

68.author: Transliteration
68.text: Waman athlamu mimmani iftara AAala Allahi kathiban aw kaththaba bialhaqqi lamma jaahu alaysa fee jahannama mathwan lilkafireena

69.author: Ahmed Ali
69.text: We shall guide those who strive in Our cause to the paths leading straight to Us. Surely God is with those who do good.

69.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
69.text: And those who strove in Our way – We shall surely show them Our paths; and indeed Allah is with the virtuous.

69.author: Arberry
69.text: But those who struggle in Our cause, surely We shall guide them in Our ways; and God is with the good-doers.

69.author: Daryabadi
69.text: And those who strive hard in us, We shall surely guide them in Our paths; verily Allah is with the welldoers.

69.author: Hilali & Khan
69.text: As for those who strive hard in Us (Our Cause), We will surely guide them to Our Paths (i.e. Allah's Religion - Islamic Monotheism). And verily, Allah is with the Muhsinun (good doers)."

69.author: Itani
69.text: As for those who strive for Us—We will guide them in Our ways. God is with the doers of good.

69.author: Maududi
69.text: As for those who strive in Our cause, We shall surely guide them to Our Ways. Indeed Allah is with those who do good.

69.author: Mubarakpuri
69.text: As for those who strive hard for Us, We will surely guide them to Our paths. And verily, Allah is with the doers of good.

69.author: Pickthall
69.text: As for those who strive in Us, We surely guide them to Our paths, and lo! Allah is with the good.

69.author: Qarai
69.text: As for those who strive in Us, We shall surely guide them in Our ways, and Allah is indeed with the virtuous.

69.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
69.text: Those who struggle in Our cause, We will surely guide them to Our ways; and Allah is with those who do good.

69.author: Saheeh International
69.text: And those who strive for Us - We will surely guide them to Our ways. And indeed, Allah is with the doers of good.

69.author: Sarwar
69.text: We shall certainly guide those who strive for Our cause to Our path. God is certainly with the righteous ones.

69.author: Shakir
69.text: And (as for) those who strive hard for Us, We will most certainly guide them in Our ways; and Allah is most surely with the doers of good.

69.author: Wahiduddin Khan
69.text: We will surely guide in Our ways those who strive hard for Our cause, God is surely with the righteous.

69.author: Yusuf Ali
69.text: And those who strive in Our (cause),- We will certainly guide them to our Paths: For verily Allah is with those who do right.

69.author: Transliteration
69.text: Waallatheena jahadoo feena lanahdiyannahum subulana wainna Allaha lamaAAa almuhsineena
